name: pyedb

metadata.name: pyedb
metadata.summary: Higher-Level Pythonic Ansys Electronics Data Base

documentation:
# PyEDB [![PyAnsys](https://img.shields.io/badge/Py-Ansys-ffc107.svg?logo=data:image/png;base64,)](https://docs.pyansys.com/) [![PythonVersion](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.8+-blue.svg)](https://www.python.org/downloads/) [![MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) ## What is PyEDB? PyEDB is Python client library for processing complex and large layout designs in the Ansys Electronics Database (EDB) format, which stores information describing designs for [Ansys Electronics Desktop](https://www.ansys.com/products/electronics) (AEDT). While you can also use the [PyEDB-Core](https://github.com/ansys/pyedb-core) API to automate EDB workflows, using it requires a deep comprehension of the EDB architecture and class inheritances, resulting in a learning curve not always compatible with daily work loads. To speed up EDB adoption and improve user experience, PyEDB provides high-level classes that call the PyEDB-Core API. Thanks to PyEDB's application-oriented architecture, you can start using EDB faster and easier. ## About PyEDB PyEDB is part of the larger [PyAnsys](https://docs.pyansys.com "PyAnsys") effort to facilitate the use of Ansys technologies directly from Python. It is intended to consolidate and extend all existing functionalities around scripting for EDB to allow reuse of existing code, sharing of best practices, and increased collaboration. PyEDB includes functionality for interacting with Ansys electromagnetic simulators: : HFSS, HFSS 3D Layout, Icepak, Maxwell, Q3D, and SIwave. ## What is EDB? EDB provides a proprietary database file format (AEDB) for efficient and fast layout design handling and processing for building ready-to-solve projects. EDB addresses signal integrity (SI), power integrity (PI-DC), and electro-thermal work flows. You can import an AEDB file into AEDT to modify the layout, assign materials, and define ports, simulations, and constraints. You can then launch any of the Ansys electromagnetic simulators. EDB runs as a standalone API, which means that you don't need to open a user interface (UI). Because EDB opens the ``aedb`` folder for directly querying and manipulating layout design in memory, it provides the fastest and most efficient way to handle a large and complex layout. You can also parse an AEDB file from a command line in batch in an Ansys electromagnetic simulator like HFSS or SIwave. Thus, you can deploy completely non-graphical flows, from layout translation through simulatiom results. Additionally, you can use PyAEDT to import an AEDB file into AEDT to view a project, combine 3D designs, or perform simulation postprocessing. EDB also supports 3D component models. ## Documentation and issues Documentation for the latest stable release of PyEDB is hosted at [PyEDB documentation](https://edb.docs.pyansys.com/version/stable/index.html). The documentation has five sections: - [Getting started](https://edb.docs.pyansys.com/version/version/stable/getting_started/index.html): Describes how to install PyEDB in user mode. - [User guide](https://edb.docs.pyansys.com/version/version/stable/user_guide/index.html): Describes how to use PyEDB. - [API reference](https://edb.docs.pyansys.com/version/version/stable/api/index.html): Provides API member descriptions and usage examples. - [Examples](https://edb.docs.pyansys.com/version/version/stable/examples/index.html): Provides examples showing end-to-end workflows for using PyEDB. - [Contribute](https://edb.docs.pyansys.com/version/version/stable/contribute.html): Describes how to install PyEDB in developer mode and how to contribute to this PyAnsys library. In the upper right corner of the documentation's title bar, there is an option for switching from viewing the documentation for the latest stable release to viewing the documentation for the development version or previously released versions. On the [PyEDB Issues](https://github.com/ansys/pyedb/issues) page, you can create issues to report bugs and request new features. On the [PyEDB Discussions](https://github.com/ansys/pyedb/discussions) page or the [Discussions](https://discuss.ansys.com/) page on the Ansys Developer portal, you can post questions, share ideas, and get community feedback. To reach the project support team, email [pyansys.core@ansys.com](mailto:pyansys.core@ansys.com). ## License PyEDB is licensed under the [MIT License](https://github.com/ansys/pyedb/blob/main/LICENSE). PyEDB makes no commercial claim over Ansys whatsoever. This library extends the functionality of EDB by adding a Python interface to PyEDB-Core without changing the core behavior or license of the original software. The use of PyEDB requires a legally licensed local copy of AEDT. To get a copy of AEDT, see the [Ansys Electronics](https://www.ansys.com/products/electronics) page on the Ansys website.

back to top

classes.name: pyedb.edb_logger.AppFilter

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.AppFilter.filter
documentation:
Modify the record sent to the logger.

Parameters
----------
record : class:`logging.LogRecord`
    Contains information related to the event being logged.

Specifies the destination of the logger.

AEDT exposes three different loggers, which are the global, project, and design loggers.

# Parameters

destination

: Logger to write to. Options are ``` "Global"`, ``"Project" ```, and `"Design"`.
  The default is `"Global"`.

extra

: Name of the design or project. The default is `""`.

classes.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.add_file_logger
documentation: Add a new file to the logger handlers list.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.remove_file_logger
documentation: Remove a file from the logger handlers list.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.remove_all_file_loggers
documentation: Remove all file loggers.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.logger
documentation: EDB logger object.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.reset_timer
documentation:
"Reset actual timer to  actual time or specified time.

Parameters
----------
time_val : float, optional
    Value time to apply.

Returns
-------

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.add_error_message
documentation:
Add a type 2 "Error" message to the message manager tree.

Also add an error message to the logger if the handler is present.

Parameters
----------
message_text : str
    Text to display as the error message.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.add_warning_message
documentation:
Add a type 1 "Warning" message to the message manager tree.

Also add a warning message to the logger if the handler is present.

# Parameters

message_text

: Text to display as the warning message.

# Examples

Add a warning message to the EDB message manager.

```pycon
>>> edb.logger.warning("Global warning message")
```

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.add_info_message
documentation:
Add a type 0 "Info" message to the active design level of the message manager tree.

Also add an info message to the logger if the handler is present.

# Parameters

message_text

: Text to display as the info message.

# Examples

Add an info message at the global level.

```pycon
>>> edb.logger.info("Global warning message")
```

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.add_debug_message
documentation:
Parameterized message to the message manager.

Parameters
----------
message_text : str
    Text to display as the message.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.add_message
documentation:
Add a message to the message manager.

# Parameters

message_type

: Type of the message. Options are:
  \* `0` : Info
  \* `1` : Warning
  \* `2` : Error
  \* `3` : Debug

message_text

: Text to display as the message.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.disable_stdout_log
documentation: Disable printing log messages to stdout.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.enable_stdout_log
documentation: Enable printing log messages to stdout.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.disable_log_on_file
documentation: Disable writing log messages to an output file.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.enable_log_on_file
documentation: Enable writing log messages to an output file.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.info
documentation: Write an info message to the global logger.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.info_timer
documentation:
Write an info message to the global logger with elapsed time.
Message will have an appendix of type Elapsed time: time.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.warning
documentation: Write a warning message to the global logger.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.error
documentation: Write an error message to the global logger.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.debug
documentation: Write a debug message to the global logger.

methods.name: pyedb.edb_logger.EdbLogger.glb
documentation: Global logger.

Specifies the logger to use for EDB logger.

This class allows you to add a handler to write messages to a file and to indicate
whether to write messages to the standard output (stdout).

# Parameters

level

: Logging level to filter the message severity allowed in the logger.
  The default is `logging.DEBUG`.

filename

: Name of the file to write messages to. The default is `None`.

to_stdout

: Whether to write log messages to stdout. The default is `False`.

methods.name: pyedb.common.nets.CommonNets.plot
documentation:
Plot a Net to Matplotlib 2D Chart.

# Parameters

nets

: Name of the net or list of nets to plot. If `None` all nets will be plotted.

layers

: Name of the layers to include in the plot. If `None` all the signal layers will be considered.

color_by_net

: If `True` the plot will be colored by net.
  If `False` the plot will be colored by layer. (default)

show_legend

: If `True` the legend is shown in the plot. (default)
  If `False` the legend is not shown.

save_plot

: If a path is specified the plot will be saved in this location.
  If `save_plot` is provided, the `show` parameter is ignored.

outline

: Add a customer outline from a list of points of the outline to plot.

size

: Image size in pixel (width, height). Default value is `(6000, 3000)`

top_view

: Whether if use top view or bottom view. Components will be visible only for the highest layer in the view.

plot_components

: If `True` the components placed on top layer are plotted.
  If `False` the components are not plotted. (default).
  This may impact in the plot computation time.
  If nets and/or layers is specified, only the components belonging to the specified nets/layers are plotted.

annotate_component_names: bool, optional

: Whether to add the component names to the plot or not. Default is `True`.

plot_vias

: Whether to plot vias (circular and rectangular) or not. This may impact in the plot computation time.
  Default is `False`.

show

: Whether to show the plot or not. Default is `True`.

include_outline

: Whether to include the internal layout outline or not. Default is `True`.

plot_edges

: Whether to plot polygon edges or not. Default is `True`.

# Returns

(ax, fig)

: Matplotlib ax and figures.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.parse_dim_arg
documentation:
Convert a number and unit to a float.
Angles are converted in radians.

# Parameters

string

: String to convert. For example, `"2mm"`. The default is `None`.

scale_to_unit

: Units for the value to convert. For example, `"mm"`.

variable_manager

: Try to parse formula and returns numeric value.
  The default is `None`.

# Returns

float

: Value for the converted value and units. For example, `0.002`.

# Examples

Parse `'"2mm"'`.

```pycon
>>> from pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators import GeometryOperators as go
>>> go.parse_dim_arg('2mm')
>>> 0.002
```

Use the optional argument `scale_to_unit` to specify the destination unit.

```pycon
>>> go.parse_dim_arg('2mm', scale_to_unit='mm')
>>> 2.0
```

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.distance_vector
documentation:
Evaluate the vector distance between point `p` and a line defined by two points, `a` and `b`.

:::{note}
he formula is `d = (a-p)-((a-p)dot p)n`, where `a` is a point of the line (either `a` or `b`)
and `n` is the unit vector in the direction of the line.
:::

# Parameters

p

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the reference point.

a

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the first point of the segment.

b

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the second point of the segment.

# Returns

List

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the distance vector.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.atan2
documentation:
Implementation of atan2 that does not suffer from the following issues:
math.atan2(0.0, 0.0) = 0.0
math.atan2(-0.0, 0.0) = -0.0
math.atan2(0.0, -0.0) = 3.141592653589793
math.atan2(-0.0, -0.0) = -3.141592653589793
and returns always 0.0.

Parameters
----------
y : float
    Y-axis value for atan2.

x : float
    X-axis value for atan2.

Returns
-------
float

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.q_prod
documentation:
Evaluate the product of two quaternions, `p` and `q`, defined as:
p = p0 + p' = p0 + ip1 + jp2 + kp3.
q = q0 + q' = q0 + iq1 + jq2 + kq3.
r = pq = p0q0 - p' • q' + p0q' + q0p' + p' x q'.

# Parameters

p

: List of `[p1, p2, p3, p4]` coordinates for quaternion `p`.

q

: List of `[p1, p2, p3, p4]` coordinates for quaternion `q`.

# Returns

List

: List of [r1, r2, r3, r4] coordinates for the result quaternion.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.q_rotation
documentation:
Evaluate the rotation of a vector, defined by a quaternion.
Evaluated as:
`"q = q0 + q' = q0 + iq1 + jq2 + kq3"`,
`"w = qvq* = (q0^2 - |q'|^2)v + 2(q' • v)q' + 2q0(q' x v)"`.

# Parameters

v

: List of `[v1, v2, v3]` coordinates for the vector.

q

: List of `[q1, q2, q3, q4]` coordinates for the quaternion.

# Returns

List

: List of `[w1, w2, w3]` coordinates for the result vector `w`.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.q_rotation_inv
documentation:
Evaluate the inverse rotation of a vector that is defined by a quaternion.

It can also be the rotation of the coordinate frame with respect to the vector.

> q = q0 + q' = q0 + iq1 + jq2 + kq3
> q\* = q0 - q' = q0 - iq1 - jq2 - kq3
> w = q\*vq

# Parameters

v

: List of `[v1, v2, v3]` coordinates for the vector.

q

: List of `[q1, q2, q3, q4]` coordinates for the quaternion.

# Returns

List

: List of `[w1, w2, w3]` coordinates for the vector.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.get_polygon_centroid
documentation:
Evaluate the centroid of a polygon defined by its points.

# Parameters

pts

: List of points, with each point defined by its `[x,y,z]` coordinates.

# Returns

List

: List of [x,y,z] coordinates for the centroid of the polygon.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.cs_xy_pointing_expression
documentation:
Return x_pointing and y_pointing vectors as expressions from
the yaw, ptich, and roll input (as strings).

Parameters
----------
yaw : str, required
    String expression for the yaw angle (rotation about Z-axis)
pitch : str
    String expression for the pitch angle (rotation about Y-axis)
roll : str
    String expression for the roll angle (rotation about X-axis)

Returns
-------
[x_pointing, y_pointing] vector expressions.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.get_numeric
documentation: Convert a string to a numeric value. Discard the suffix.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.is_small
documentation:
Return `True` if the number represented by s is zero (i.e very small).

# Parameters

s

: Variable value.

# Returns

> bool

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.numeric_cs
documentation:
Return a list of [x,y,z] numeric values given a coordinate system as input.

# Parameters

cs_in

: `["x", "y", "z"]` or "Global".

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.orient_polygon
documentation:
Orient a polygon clockwise or counterclockwise. The vertices should be already ordered either way.
Use this function to change the orientation.
The polygon is represented by its vertices coordinates.

# Parameters

x

: List of x coordinates of the vertices. Length must be >= 1.
  Degenerate polygon with only 2 points is also accepted, in this case the points are returned unchanged.

y

: List of y coordinates of the vertices. Must be of the same length as x.

clockwise

: If `True` the polygon is oriented clockwise, if `False` it is oriented counterclockwise.
  Default is `True`.

# Returns

List of List

: Lists of oriented vertices.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.v_angle_sign
documentation:
Evaluate the signed angle between two geometry vectors.
The sign is evaluated respect to the normal to the plane containing the two vectors as per the following rule.
In case of opposite vectors, it returns an angle equal to 180deg (always positive).
Assuming that the plane normal is normalized (vb == 1), the signed angle is simplified.
For the right-handed rotation from Va to Vb:
\- atan2((va x Vb) . vn, va . vb).
For the left-handed rotation from Va to Vb:
\- atan2((Vb x va) . vn, va . vb).

# Parameters

va

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the first vector.

vb

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the second vector.

vn

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the plane normal.

right_handed

: Whether to consider the right-handed rotation from va to vb. The default is `True`.
  When `False`, left-hand rotation from va to vb is considered.

# Returns

float

: Angle in radians.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.v_angle_sign_2D
documentation:
Evaluate the signed angle between two 2D geometry vectors.
Iit the 2D version of the `GeometryOperators.v_angle_sign` considering vn = [0,0,1].
In case of opposite vectors, it returns an angle equal to 180deg (always positive).

# Parameters

va

: List of `[x, y]` coordinates for the first vector.

vb

: List of `[x, y]` coordinates for the second vector.

right_handed

: Whether to consider the right-handed rotation from Va to Vb. The default is `True`.
  When `False`, left-hand rotation from Va to Vb is considered.

# Returns

float

: Angle in radians.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.point_in_polygon
documentation:
Determine if a point is inside, outside the polygon or at exactly at the border.

The method implements the radial algorithm (<https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algoritmo_radial>)

point

: List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

polygon

: \[[x1, x2, ..., xn],[y1, y2, ..., yn]\]

tolerance

: tolerance used for the algorithm. Default value is 1e-8.

# Returns

int
: - `-1` When the point is outside the polygon.
  - `0` When the point is exactly on one of the sides of the polygon.
  - `1` When the point is inside the polygon.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.is_point_in_polygon
documentation:
Determine if a point is inside or outside a polygon, both located on the same plane.

The method implements the radial algorithm (<https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algoritmo_radial>)

point

: List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

polygon

: \[[x1, x2, ..., xn],[y1, y2, ..., yn]\]

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the point is inside the polygon or exactly on one of its sides.
  `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.are_segments_intersecting
documentation:
Determine if the two segments a and b are intersecting.

a1

: First point of segment a. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

a2

: Second point of segment a. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

b1

: First point of segment b. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

b2

: Second point of segment b. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

include_collinear

: If `True` two segments are considered intersecting also if just one end lies on the other segment.
  Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the segments are intersecting.
  `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.is_segment_intersecting_polygon
documentation:
Determine if a segment defined by two points `a` and `b` intersects a polygon.
Points on the vertices and on the polygon boundaries are not considered intersecting.

a

: First point of the segment. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

b

: Second point of the segment. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

polygon

: \[[x1, x2, ..., xn],[y1, y2, ..., yn]\]

# Returns

float

: `True` if the segment intersect the polygon. `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.is_perpendicular
documentation:
Check if two vectors are perpendicular.

# Parameters

a

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the first vector.

b

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the second vector.

tol

: Linear tolerance. The default value is `1e-6`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if vectors are perpendicular, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.is_point_projection_in_segment
documentation:
Check if a point projection lies on the segment defined by two points.

# Parameters

p

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the reference point `p`.

a

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the first point of the segment.

b

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the second point of the segment.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when the projection point lies on the segment defined by the two points, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.point_segment_distance
documentation:
Calculate the distance between a point `p` and a segment defined by two points `a` and `b`.

# Parameters

p

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the reference point `p`.

a

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the first point of the segment.

b

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the second point of the segment.

# Returns

float

: Distance between the point and the segment.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.find_largest_rectangle_inside_polygon
documentation:
Find the largest area rectangles of arbitrary orientation in a polygon.

Implements the algorithm described by Rubén Molano, et al.
*"Finding the largest area rectangle of arbitrary orientation in a closed contour"*, published in
*Applied Mathematics and Computation*.
<https://doi.org/10.1016/j.amc.2012.03.063>.
(<https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0096300312003207>)

# Parameters

polygon

: \[[x1, x2, ..., xn],[y1, y2, ..., yn]\]

partition_max_order

: Order of the lattice partition used to find the quasi-lattice polygon that approximates `polygon`.
  Default is `16`.

# Returns

List of List

: List containing the rectangles points. Return all rectangles found.
  List is in the form: \[\[[x1, y1],[x2, y2],...\],\[[x1, y1],[x2, y2],...\],...\].

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.degrees_over_rounded
documentation:
Ceil of angle.

Parameters
----------
angle : float
    Angle in radians which will be converted to degrees and will be over-rounded to the next "digits" decimal.
digits : int
    Integer number which is the number of decimals.

Returns
-------
float

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.radians_over_rounded
documentation:
Radian angle ceiling.

Parameters
----------
angle : float
    Angle in degrees which will be converted to radians and will be over-rounded to the  next "digits" decimal.
digits : int
    Integer number which is the number of decimals.

Returns
-------
float

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.degrees_default_rounded
documentation:
Convert angle to degree with given digits rounding.

Parameters
----------
angle : float
    Angle in radians which will be converted to degrees and will be under-rounded to the next "digits" decimal.
digits : int
    Integer number which is the number of decimals.

Returns
-------
float

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.radians_default_rounded
documentation:
Convert to radians with given round.

Parameters
----------
angle : float
    Angle in degrees which will be converted to radians and will be under-rounded to the next "digits" decimal.
digits : int
    Integer number which is the number of decimals.

Returns
-------
float

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.find_closest_points
documentation:
Given a list of points, finds the closest points to a reference point.
It returns a list of points because more than one can be found.
It works with 2D or 3D points. The tolerance used to evaluate the distance
to the reference point can be specified.

# Parameters

points_list

: List of points. The points can be defined in 2D or 3D space.

reference_point

: The reference point. The point can be defined in 2D or 3D space (same as points_list).

tol

: The tolerance used to evaluate the distance. Default is `1e-6`.

# Returns

List of List

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.mirror_point
documentation:
Mirror point about a plane defining by a point on the plane and a normal point.

Parameters
----------
start : list
    Point to be mirrored
reference : list
    The reference point. Point on the plane around which you want to mirror the object.
vector : list
    Normalized vector used for the mirroring.

Returns
-------
List
    List of the reflected point.

methods.name: pyedb.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.find_points_along_lines
documentation:
Detect all points that are placed along lines.

The method takes as input a list of 2D points and detects all lines that contain at least 3 points.
Optionally, the minimum number of points contained in a line can be specified by setting the
argument `minimum_number_of_points`.
As default, all points along the lines are returned, regardless of their relative distance.
Optionally, a `distance_threshold` can be set. If two points in a line are separated by a distance larger than
`distance_threshold`, the line is divided in two parts. If one of those parts does not satisfy the
`minimum_number_of_points` requirement, it is discarded.
If `distance_threshold` is set (not `None`), the computational time increases.

points

: The points to process. Can be a list of lists where each sublist
  represents a 2D point `[x, y]` coordinates, or a numpy array of shape (n, 2).

minimum_number_of_points

: The minimum number of points that a line must contain. Default is `3`.

distance_threshold

: If two points in a line are separated by a distance larger than `distance_threshold`,
  the line is divided in two parts. Default is `None`, in which case the control is not performed.

selected_angles

: Specify a list of angles in degrees. If specified, the method returns only the lines which
  have one of the specified angles.
  The angle is defined as the positive angle of the infinite line with respect to the x-axis
  It is positive, and between 0 and 180 degrees.
  For example, `[90]` indicated vertical lines parallel to the y-axis,
  `[0]` or `[180]` identifies horizontal lines parallel to the x-axis,
  `45` identifies lines parallel to the first quadrant bisector.
  Default is `None`, in which case all lines are returned.

return_additional_info

: Whether to return additional information about the number of elements processed.
  The default is `True`.

# Returns

tuple

: The tuple contains:
  \- lines: a list of lists where each sublist represents a 2D point `[x, y]` coordinates in each line.
  \- lines indexes: a list of lists where each sublist represents the index of the point in each line.

  > The index is referring to the point position in the input point list.

  - number of processed points: optional, returned if `return_additional_info` is `True`
  - number of processed lines: optional, returned if `return_additional_info` is `True`
  - number of detected lines after `minimum_number_of_points` is applied: optional,
    : returned if `return_additional_info` is `True`
  - number of detected lines after `distance_threshold` is applied: optional,
    : returned if `return_additional_info` is `True`
Manages geometry operators.

classes.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationSetup

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationSetup.create
documentation: Create an HFSS setup.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationSetup.add_frequency_sweep
documentation:
Add frequency sweep.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the frequency sweep.

frequency_sweep

: List of frequency points.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.simulation_setup.EdbFrequencySweep`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> setup1 = edbapp.create_hfss_setup("setup1")
>>> setup1.add_frequency_sweep(frequency_sweep=[
...                           ["linear count", "0", "1kHz", 1],
...                           ["log scale", "1kHz", "0.1GHz", 10],
...                           ["linear scale", "0.1GHz", "10GHz", "0.1GHz"],
...                           ])
```
Manages EDB methods for RaptorX simulation setup.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXGeneralSettings.global_temperature
documentation: The simulation temperature. Units: C

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXGeneralSettings.max_frequency
documentation:
This allows user to specify the maximum simulation frequency, a parameter which controls how tight the model
mesh will be. User can override the default meshing frequency as defined by Max Frequency using the Advanced
settings > MeshFrequency. Example: "10GHz".

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.cell_per_wave_length
documentation:
This setting describes the number of cells that fit under each wavelength. The wavelength is
calculated according to the Max Frequency or the Mesh Frequency, unless specified by user through
this setting. E.g. Setting Cells/Wavelength to 20 means that an object will be divided into 10 cells
if its width or length is 1/2 wavelengths.
Units: unitless.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.edge_mesh
documentation:
This option controls both, the thickness and the width of the exterior conductor filament.
When specified, it prevails over the Mesh Frequency or Max Frequency during mesh calculation.
Example: "0.8um".

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.eliminate_slit_per_hole
documentation:
This is a setting that internally simplifies layouts with strain relief or thermal relief slits and
holes. It will examine each hole separately against the whole polygon it belongs to.
If the area of the hole is below the threshold defined in this setting, then the hole will be filled.
Units: unitless.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.mesh_frequency
documentation:
User can override the default meshing applied by setting a custom frequency for mesh generation.
Example: "1GHz".

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.net_settings_options
documentation: A list of Name, Value pairs that stores advanced option.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.override_shrink_fac
documentation:
Set the shrink factor explicitly, that is, review what-if scenarios of migrating to half-node
technologies.
Units: unitless.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.plane_projection_factor
documentation:
To eliminate unnecessary mesh complexity of "large" metal planes and improve overall extraction time,
user can define the mesh of certain planes using a combination of the Plane Projection Factor and
settings of the Nets Advanced Options.
Units: unitless.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_accelerate_via_extraction
documentation:
Setting this option will simplify/merge neighboring vias before sending the layout for processing
to the mesh engine and to the EM engine.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_auto_removal_sliver_poly
documentation: Setting this option simplifies layouts by aligning slightly misaligned overlapping polygons.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_cells_per_wavelength
documentation:
This setting describes the number of cells that fit under each wavelength. The wavelength is calculated
according to the Max Frequency or the Mesh Frequency, unless specified by user through this setting.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_edge_mesh
documentation:
This option controls both, the thickness and the width of the exterior conductor filament.
When checked, it prevails over the Mesh Frequency or Max Frequency during mesh calculation.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_eliminate_slit_per_holes
documentation:
This is a setting that internally simplifies layouts with strain relief or thermal relief slits and
holes.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_enable_advanced_cap_effects
documentation:
Applies all the capacitance related effects such as Conformal Dielectrics, Loading Effect,
Dielectric Damage.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_enable_etch_transform
documentation:
Pre-distorts the layout based on the foundry rules, applying the conductor's bias (positive/negative –
deflation/inflation) at the conductor edges due to unavoidable optical effects in the manufacturing process.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_enable_hybrid_extraction
documentation:
This setting allows the modelling engine to separate the layout into two parts in an attempt to
decrease the complexity of EM modelling.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_enable_substrate_network_extraction
documentation: This setting models substrate coupling effects using an equivalent distributed RC network.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_extract_floating_metals_dummy
documentation:
Enables modeling of floating metals as dummy fills. Captures the effect of dummy fill by extracting
the effective capacitance between any pairs of metal segments in the design, in the presence of each
individual dummy metal islands. This setting cannot be used with UseExtractFloatingMetalsFloating.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_extract_floating_metals_floating
documentation:
Enables modeling of floating metals as floating nets. Floating metal are grouped into a single entity
and treated as an independent net. This setting cannot be used with UseExtractFloatingMetalsDummy.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_lde
documentation:
Takes into account the variation of resistivity as a function of a conductor’s drawn width and spacing to
its neighbors or as a function of its local density, due to dishing, slotting, cladding thickness, and so
on.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_mesh_frequency
documentation:
User can override the default meshing applied by the mesh engine by checking this option and setting a
custom frequency for mesh generation.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_override_shrink_fac
documentation:
Set the shrink factor explicitly, that is, review what-if scenarios of migrating to half-node
technologies.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_plane_projection_factor
documentation:
To eliminate unnecessary mesh complexity of "large" metal planes and improve overall
extraction time, user can define the mesh of certain planes using a combination of the Plane Projection
Factor and settings of the Nets Advanced Options.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.raptor_x_simulation_setup_data.RaptorXSimulationAdvancedSettings.use_relaxed_z_axis
documentation: Enabling this option provides a simplified mesh along the z-axis.

huray_radius

: Radius of huray model. The default is `"0.5um"`.

huray_surface_ratio

: Surface ratio of huray model. The default is `"2.9"`.

groisse_roughness

: Roughness of groisse model. The default is `"1um"`.

apply_on_surface
: Where to assign roughness model. The default is `"all"`. Options are `"top"`, `"bottom"`,

  : `"side"`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edbapp = ansys.aedt.core.Edb("myproject.aedb")

>>> for polygon in top_layer_polygon:

```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.primitives_data.EdbPolygon.polygon_data
documentation: {class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.dotnet.database.PolygonDataDotNet`: Outer contour of the Polygon object.
def add_void(self, point_list):
"""Add a void to current primitive.
Parameters
point_list : list or  :class:`dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.primitives_data.Primitive` or EDB Primitive Object
Point list in the format of `[[x1,y1], [x2,y2],..,[xn,yn]]`.
Returns
bool
``True`` if successful, either  ``False``.
if isinstance(point_list, list):
plane = self._app.modeler.Shape("polygon", points=point_list)
_poly = self._app.modeler.shape_to_polygon_data(plane)
if _poly is None or _poly.IsNull() or _poly is False:
self._logger.error("Failed to create void polygon data")
return False
prim = self._app.edb_api.cell.primitive.polygon.create(
self._app.active_layout, self.layer_name, self.primitive_object.GetNet(), _poly
elif isinstance(point_list, Primitive):
prim = point_list.primitive_object
else:
prim = point_list
return self.add_void(prim)

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.primitives_data.EdbPolygon.expand
documentation:
Expand the polygon shape by an absolute value in all direction.
Offset can be negative for negative expansion.

Parameters
----------
offset : float, optional
    Offset value in meters.
tolerance : float, optional
    Tolerance in meters.
round_corners : bool, optional
    Whether to round corners or not.
    If True, use rounded corners in the expansion otherwise use straight edges (can be degenerate).
maximum_corner_extension : float, optional
    The maximum corner extension (when round corners are not used) at which point the corner is clipped.

modules.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables
documentation:
This module contains these classes: `CSVDataset`, `DataSet`, `Expression`, `Variable`, and `VariableManager`.

This module is used to create and edit design and project variables in the 3D tools.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss["$d"] = "5mm"
>>> hfss["d"] = "5mm"
>>> hfss["postd"] = "1W"
```

functions.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.generate_validation_errors
documentation:
From the given property names, expected settings and actual settings, return a list of validation errors.
If no errors are found, an empty list is returned. The validation of values such as "10mm"
ensures that they are close to within a relative tolerance.
For example an expected setting of "10mm", and actual of "10.000000001mm" will not yield a validation error.
For values with no numerical value, an equivalence check is made.

Parameters
----------
property_names : List[str]
    List of property names.
expected_settings : List[str]
    List of the expected settings.
actual_settings : List[str]
    List of actual settings.

Returns
-------
List[str]
    A list of validation errors for the given settings.

documentation: Create an iterator to yield the row data as a string as we loop through the object

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.CSVDataset.next
documentation: Yield the next row.

Reads in a CSV file and extracts data, which can be augmented with constant values.

# Parameters

csv_file

: Input file consisting of delimited data with the first line as the header.
  The CSV value includes the header and data, which supports AEDT units information
  such as `"1.23Wb"`. You can also augment the data with constant values.

separator

: Value to use for the delimiter. The default is\`\`None\`\` in which case a comma is
  assumed.

units_dict

: Dictionary consisting of `{Variable Name: unit}` to rescale the data
  if it is not in the desired unit system.

append_dict

: Dictionary consisting of `{New Variable Name: value}` to add variables
  with constant values to all data points. This dictionary is used to add
  multiple sweeps to one result file.

valid_solutions

: The default is `True`.

invalid_solutions

: The default is `False`.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.VariableManager.decompose
documentation:
Decompose a variable string to a floating with its unit.

# Parameters

variable_value : str

# Returns

tuple

: The float value of the variable and the units exposed as a string.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> print(hfss.variable_manager.decompose("5mm"))
>>> (5.0, 'mm')
>>> hfss["v1"] = "3N"
>>> print(hfss.variable_manager.decompose("v1"))
>>> (3.0, 'N')
>>> hfss["v2"] = "2*v1"
>>> print(hfss.variable_manager.decompose("v2"))
>>> (6.0, 'N')
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.VariableManager.set_variable
documentation:
Set the value of a design property or project variable.

# Parameters

variable_name

: Name of the design property or project variable
  (`$var`). If this variable does not exist, a new one is
  created and a value is set.

expression

: Valid string expression within the AEDT design and project
  structure. For example, `"3*cos(34deg)"`.

readonly

: Whether to set the design property or project variable to
  read-only. The default is `False`.

hidden

: Whether to hide the design property or project variable. The
  default is `False`.

description

: Text to display for the design property or project variable in the
  `Properties` window. The default is `None`.

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing value for the design
  property or project variable. The default is `False`, in
  which case this method is ignored.

postprocessing
: Whether to define a postprocessing variable.

  : The default is `False`, in which case the variable is not used in postprocessing.

circuit_parameter
: Whether to define a parameter in a circuit design or a local parameter.

  : The default is `True`, in which case a circuit variable is created as a parameter default.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oProject.ChangeProperty
>>> oDesign.ChangeProperty
```

# Examples

Set the value of design property `p1` to `"10mm"`,
creating the property if it does not already eixst.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.variable_manager.set_variable("p1", expression="10mm")
```

Set the value of design property `p1` to `"20mm"` only if
the property does not already exist.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.variable_manager.set_variable("p1", expression="20mm", overwrite=False)
```

Set the value of design property `p2` to `"10mm"`,
creating the property if it does not already exist. Also make
it read-only and hidden and add a description.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.variable_manager.set_variable(variable_name="p2", expression="10mm", readonly=True, hidden=True,
...                                       description="This is the description of this variable.")
```

Set the value of the project variable `$p1` to `"30mm"`,
creating the variable if it does not exist.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.variable_manager.set_variable["$p1"] == "30mm"
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.VariableManager.delete_separator
documentation:
Delete a separator from either the active project or design.

# Parameters

separator_name

: Value to use for the delimiter.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.VariableManager.delete_variable
documentation:
Delete a variable.

# Parameters

var_name

: Name of the variable.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oProject.ChangeProperty
>>> oDesign.ChangeProperty
```

Manages design properties and project variables.

Design properties are the local variables in a design. Project
variables are defined at the project level and start with `$`.

This class provides access to all variables or a subset of the
variables. Manipulation of the numerical or string definitions of
variable values is provided in the
{class}`pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable` class.

# Parameters

variables

: Dictionary of all design properties and project variables in
  the active design.

design_variables

: Dictionary of all design properties in the active design.

project_variables

: Dictionary of all project variables available to the active
  design (key by variable name).

dependent_variables

: Dictionary of all dependent variables available to the active
  design (key by variable name).

independent_variables

: Dictionary of all independent variables (constant numeric
  values) available to the active design (key by variable name).

independent_design_variables : dict

independent_project_variables : dict

variable_names

: One or more variable names.

project_variable_names

: One or more project variable names.

design_variable_names

: One or more design variable names.

dependent_variable_names

: All dependent variable names within the project.

independent_variable_names

: All independent variable names within the project. These can
  be sweep variables for optimetrics.

independent_project_variable_names

: All independent project variable names within the
  project. These can be sweep variables for optimetrics.

independent_design_variable_names

: All independent design properties (local variables) within the
  project. These can be sweep variables for optimetrics.

# See Also

pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.maxwell import Maxwell3d
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.desktop import Desktop
>>> d = Desktop()
>>> aedtapp = Maxwell3d()
```

Define some test variables.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp["Var1"] = 3
>>> aedtapp["Var2"] = "12deg"
>>> aedtapp["Var3"] = "Var1 * Var2"
>>> aedtapp["$PrjVar1"] = "pi"
```

Get the variable manager for the active design.

```pycon
>>> v = aedtapp.variable_manager
```

Get a dictionary of all project and design variables.

```pycon
>>> v.variables
{'Var1': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable at 0x2661f34c448>,
 'Var2': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable at 0x2661f34c308>,
 'Var3': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Expression at 0x2661f34cb48>,
 '$PrjVar1': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Expression at 0x2661f34cc48>}
```

Get a dictionary of only the design variables.

```pycon
>>> v.design_variables
{'Var1': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable at 0x2661f339508>,
 'Var2': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable at 0x2661f3415c8>,
 'Var3': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Expression at 0x2661f341808>}
```

Get a dictionary of only the independent design variables.

```pycon
>>> v.independent_design_variables
{'Var1': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable at 0x2661f335d08>,
 'Var2': <pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable at 0x2661f3557c8>}
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.evaluated_value
documentation:
String value.

The numeric value with the unit is concatenated and returned as a string. The numeric display
in the modeler and the string value can differ. For example, you might see `10mm` in the
modeler and see `10.0mm` returned as the string value.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.decompose
documentation:
Decompose a variable value to a floating with its unit.

# Returns

tuple

: The float value of the variable and the units exposed as a string.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss["v1"] = "3N"
>>> print(hfss.variable_manager["v1"].decompose("v1"))
>>> (3.0, 'N')
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.rescale_to
documentation:
Rescale the expression to a new unit within the current unit system.

# Parameters

units

: Units to rescale to.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables import Variable
```

```pycon
>>> v = Variable("10W")
>>> assert v.numeric_value == 10
>>> assert v.units == "W"
>>> v.rescale_to("kW")
>>> assert v.numeric_value == 0.01
>>> assert v.units == "kW"
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.format
documentation:
Retrieve the string value with the specified numerical formatting.

# Parameters

format

: Format for the numeric value of the string. For example, `'06.2f'`. For
  more information, see the [PyFormat documentation](https://pyformat.info/).

# Returns

str

: String value with the specified numerical formatting.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables import Variable
```

```pycon
>>> v = Variable("10W")
>>> assert v.format("f") == '10.000000W'
>>> assert v.format("06.2f") == '010.00W'
>>> assert v.format("6.2f") == ' 10.00W'
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.__mul__
documentation:
Multiply the variable with a number or another variable and return a new object.

# Parameters

other

: Object to be multiplied.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables import Variable
```

Multiply `'Length1'` by unitless `` 'None'` `` to obtain `'Length'`.
A numerical value is also considered to be unitless.

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> v1 = Variable("10mm")
>>> v2 = Variable(3)
>>> result_1 = v1 * v2
>>> result_2 = v1 * 3
>>> assert result_1.numeric_value == 30.0
>>> assert result_1.unit_system == "Length"
>>> assert result_2.numeric_value == result_1.numeric_value
>>> assert result_2.unit_system == "Length"
```

Multiply voltage times current to obtain power.

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> v3 = Variable("3mA")
>>> v4 = Variable("40V")
>>> result_3 = v3 * v4
>>> assert result_3.numeric_value == 0.12
>>> assert result_3.units == "W"
>>> assert result_3.unit_system == "Power"
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.__add__
documentation:
Add the variable to another variable to return a new object.

# Parameters

other

: Object to be multiplied.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables import Variable
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> v1 = Variable("3mA")
>>> v2 = Variable("10A")
>>> result = v1 + v2
>>> assert result.numeric_value == 10.003
>>> assert result.units == "A"
>>> assert result.unit_system == "Current"
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.__sub__
documentation:
Subtract another variable from the variable to return a new object.

# Parameters

other

: Object to be subtracted.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> from pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables import Variable
>>> v3 = Variable("3mA")
>>> v4 = Variable("10A")
>>> result_2 = v3 - v4
>>> assert result_2.numeric_value == -9.997
>>> assert result_2.units == "A"
>>> assert result_2.unit_system == "Current"
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.__truediv__
documentation:
Divide the variable by a number or another variable to return a new object.

# Parameters

other

: Object by which to divide.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

Divide a variable with units `"W"` by a variable with units `"V"` and automatically
resolve the new units to `"A"`.

```pycon
>>> from pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables import Variable
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> v1 = Variable("10W")
>>> v2 = Variable("40V")
>>> result = v1 / v2
>>> assert result_1.numeric_value == 0.25
>>> assert result_1.units == "A"
>>> assert result_1.unit_system == "Current"
```
Python 3.x version

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.__div__
documentation: Python 2.7 version

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables.Variable.__rtruediv__
documentation:
Divide another object by this object.

# Parameters

other

: Object to divide by.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

Divide a number by a variable with units `"s"` and automatically determine that
the result is in `"Hz"`.

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> from pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables import Variable
>>> v = Variable("1s")
>>> result = 3.0 / v
>>> assert result.numeric_value == 3.0
>>> assert result.units == "Hz"
>>> assert result.unit_system == "Freq"
```

Stores design properties and project variables and provides operations to perform on them.

# Parameters

value

: Numerical value of the variable in SI units.

units

: Units for the value.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb.dotnet.application.Variables import Variable
```

Define a variable using a string value consistent with the AEDT properties.

```pycon
>>> v = Variable("45mm")
```

Define an unitless variable with a value of 3.0.

```pycon
>>> v = Variable(3.0)
```

Define a variable defined by a numeric result and a unit string.

```pycon
>>> v = Variable(3.0 * 4.5, units="mm")
>>> assert v.numeric_value = 13.5
>>> assert v.units = "mm"
```

Parameters

edbversion : str,  optional
        Edb version to use. Default is `None` to use latest available version of Edb.
control_file : str,  optional

Returns

str
    Control file full path if created.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.control_file.ControlFileStackup.add_layer
documentation:
Add a new layer.

# Parameters

layer_name

: Layer name.

elevation

: Layer elevation.

material

: Material for the layer.

gds_type

: GDS type assigned on the layer. The value must be the same as in the GDS file otherwise geometries won't be
  imported.

target_layer

: Layer name assigned in EDB or HFSS 3D layout after import.

thickness

: Layer thickness

layer_type

: Define the layer type, default value for a layer is `"conductor"`

solve_inside

: When `True` solver will solve inside metal, and not id `False`. Default value is `True`.

properties

: Dictionary with key and property value.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.control_file.ControlFileLayer`

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.control_file.ControlFileStackup.add_dielectric
documentation:
Add a new dielectric.

# Parameters

layer_name

: Layer name.

layer_index

: Dielectric layer index as they must be stacked. If not provided the layer index will be incremented.

material

: Material name.

thickness

: Layer thickness.

properties

: Dictionary with key and property value.

base_layer

: Layer name used for layer placement. Default value is `None`. This option is used for inserting
  dielectric layer between two existing ones. When no argument is provided the dielectric layer will be placed
  on top of the stacked ones.

method

: Provides the method to use when the argument `base_layer` is provided. When `True` the layer is added
  on top on the base layer, when `False` it will be added below.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.control_file.ControlFileDielectric`

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.control_file.ControlFileStackup.add_via
documentation:
Add a new via layer.

# Parameters

layer_name

: Layer name.

material

: Define the material for this layer.

gds_type

: Define the gds type.

target_layer

: Target layer used after layout import in EDB and HFSS 3D layout.

start_layer

: Define the start layer for the via

stop_layer

: Define the stop layer for the via.

solve_inside

: When `True` solve inside this layer is anbled. Default value is `True`.

via_group_method

: Define the via group method, default value is `"proximity"`

via_group_tol

: Define the via group tolerance.

via_group_persistent

: When `True` activated otherwise when ``` False``is deactivated. Default value is ``True ```.

snap_via_group_method

: Define the via group method, default value is `"distance"`

snap_via_group_tol

: Define the via group tolerance, default value is 10e-9.

properties

: Dictionary with key and property value.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.control_file.ControlFileVia`
Class that manages the Stackup info.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.padstacks_data.EDBPadProperties._update_pad_parameters_parameters
documentation:
Update padstack parameters.

# Parameters

layer_name

: Name of the layer. The default is `None`.

pad_type

: Type of the pad. The default is `None`.

geom_type

: Type of the geometry. The default is `None`.

params

: The default is `None`.

offsetx

: Offset value for the X axis. The default is `None`.

offsety

: Offset value for the Y axis. The default is `None`.

rotation

: Rotation value. The default is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

Manages EDB functionalities for pad properties.

# Parameters

edb_padstack :

layer_name

: Name of the layer.

pad_type :

: Type of the pad.

pedbpadstack

: Inherited AEDT object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb(myedb, edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb_pad_properties = edb.padstacks.definitions["MyPad"].pad_by_layer["TOP"]
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.padstacks_data.EDBPadstack.convert_to_3d_microvias
documentation:
Convert actual padstack instance to microvias 3D Objects with a given aspect ratio.

# Parameters

convert_only_signal_vias

: Either to convert only vias belonging to signal nets or all vias. Defaults is `True`.

hole_wall_angle

: Angle of laser penetration in degrees. The angle defines the lowest hole diameter with this formula:
  HoleDiameter -2\*tan(laser_angle\* Hole depth). Hole depth is the height of the via (dielectric thickness).
  The default is `15`.
  The lowest hole is `0.75*HoleDepth/HoleDiam`.

delete_padstack_def

: Whether to delete the padstack definition. The default is `True`.
  If `False`, the padstack definition is not deleted and the hole size is set to zero.

# Returns

> `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.padstacks_data.EDBPadstackInstance.create_rectangle_in_pad
documentation:
Create a rectangle inscribed inside a padstack instance pad.

The rectangle is fully inscribed in the pad and has the maximum area.
It is necessary to specify the layer on which the rectangle will be created.

# Parameters

layer_name

: Name of the layer on which to create the polygon.

return_points

: If `True` does not create the rectangle and just returns a list containing the rectangle vertices.
  Default is `False`.

partition_max_order

: Order of the lattice partition used to find the quasi-lattice polygon that approximates `polygon`.
  Default is `16`.

# Returns

bool, List, {class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.primitives.EDBPrimitives`

: Polygon when successful, `False` when failed, list of list if `return_points=True`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb_layout = edbapp.modeler
>>> list_of_padstack_instances = list(edbapp.padstacks.instances.values())
>>> padstack_inst = list_of_padstack_instances[0]
>>> padstack_inst.create_rectangle_in_pad("TOP")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.padstacks_data.EDBPadstackInstance.get_reference_pins
documentation:
Search for reference pins using given criteria.

# Parameters

reference_net

: Reference net. The default is `"GND"`.

search_radius

: Search radius for finding padstack instances. The default is `5e-3`.

max_limit

: Maximum limit for the padstack instances found. The default is `0`, in which
  case no limit is applied. The maximum limit value occurs on the nearest
  reference pins from the positive one that is found.

component_only

: Whether to limit the search to component padstack instances only. The
  default is `True`. When `False`, the search is extended to the entire layout.

# Returns

list

: List of {class}`dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.padstacks_data.EDBPadstackInstance`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edbapp = Edb("target_path")
>>> pin = edbapp.components.instances["J5"].pins["19"]
>>> reference_pins = pin.get_reference_pins(reference_net="GND", search_radius=5e-3, max_limit=0,
>>> component_only=True)
```

Manages EDB functionalities for a padstack.

# Parameters

edb_padstackinstance :

\_pedb :

: Inherited AEDT object.

# Examples

```pycon

>>> edb = Edb(myedb, edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb_padstack_instance = edb.padstacks.instances[0]
```

path. This is used especially to preserve the initial project when several files have to be build based on

you want to preserve the project make sure you provide the full path for the new aedb folder.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.simulation_configuration.SimulationConfiguration.add_dc_ground_source_term
documentation:
Add a dc ground source terminal for Siwave.

# Parameters

source_name

: The source name to assign the reference node to.
  Default value is `None`.

node_to_ground

: Value must be `0`: unspecified, `1`: negative node, `2`: positive node.
  Default value is `1`.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.simulation_configuration.SimulationConfiguration.export_json
documentation:
Export Json file from SimulationConfiguration object.

# Parameters

output_file

: Json file name.

# Returns

bool

: True when succeeded False when file name not provided.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.simulation_configuration import SimulationConfiguration
>>> config = SimulationConfiguration()
>>> config.export_json(r"C:\Temp        est_json        est.json")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.simulation_configuration.SimulationConfiguration.add_voltage_source
documentation:
Add a voltage source for the current SimulationConfiguration instance.

# Parameters

name

: Source name.

voltage_value

: Amplitude value of the source. Either amperes for current source or volts for
  voltage source.

phase_value

: Phase value of the source.

impedance

: Impedance value of the source.

positive_node_component

: Name of the component used for the positive node.

negative_node_component

: Name of the component used for the negative node.

positive_node_net

: Net used for the positive node.

negative_node_net

: Net used for the negative node.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb = Edb(target_file)
>>> sim_setup = SimulationConfiguration()
>>> sim_setup.add_voltage_source(voltage_value=1.0, phase_value=0, positive_node_component="V1",
>>> positive_node_net="HSG", negative_node_component="V1", negative_node_net="SW")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.simulation_configuration.SimulationConfiguration.add_rlc
documentation:
Add a voltage source for the current SimulationConfiguration instance.

# Parameters

name

: Source name.

r_value

: Resistor value in Ohms.

l_value

: Inductance value in Henry.

c_value

: Capacitance value in Farrad.

positive_node_component

: Name of the component used for the positive node.

negative_node_component

: Name of the component used for the negative node.

positive_node_net

: Net used for the positive node.

negative_node_net

: Net used for the negative node.

create_physical_rlc

: When True create a physical Rlc component. Recommended setting to True to be compatible with Siwave.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb = Edb(target_file)
>>> sim_setup = SimulationConfiguration()
>>> sim_setup.add_voltage_source(voltage_value=1.0, phase_value=0, positive_node_component="V1",
>>> positive_node_net="HSG", negative_node_component="V1", negative_node_net="SW")
```

Provides an ASCII simulation configuration file parser.

This parser supports all types of inputs for setting up and automating any kind
of SI or PI simulation with HFSS 3D Layout or Siwave. If fields are omitted, default
values are applied. This class can be instantiated directly from
Configuration file.

# Examples

This class is very convenient to build HFSS and SIwave simulation projects from layout.
It is leveraging EDB commands from Pyaedt but with keeping high level parameters making more easy PCB automation
flow. SYZ and DC simulation can be addressed with this class.

The class is instantiated from an open edb:

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb()
>>> sim_setup = edb.new_simulation_configuration()
```

The returned object sim_setup is a SimulationConfiguration object.
From this class you can assign a lot of parameters related the project configuration but also solver options.
Here is the list of parameters available:

```pycon
>>> from dotnet.generic.constants import SolverType
>>> sim_setup.solver_type = SolverType.Hfss3dLayout
```

Solver type can be selected, HFSS 3D Layout and Siwave are supported.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.signal_nets = ["net1", "net2"]
```

Set the list of net names you want to include for the simulation. These nets will
have excitations ports created if corresponding pins are found on selected component. We usually refer to signal
nets but power / reference nets can also be passed into this list if user wants to have ports created on these ones.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.power_nets = ["gnd", "vcc"]
```

Set the list on power and reference nets. These nets won't have excitation ports created
on them and will be clipped during the project build if the cutout option is enabled.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.components = ["comp1", "comp2"]
```

Set the list of components which will be included in the simulation. These components will have ports created on
pins belonging to the net list.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.do_cutout_subdesign = True
```

When true activates the layout cutout based on net signal net selection and cutout expansion.

```pycon
>>> from dotnet.generic.constants import CutoutSubdesignType
>>> sim_setup.cutout_subdesign_type = CutoutSubdesignType.Conformal
```

Define the type of cutout used for computing the clippingextent polygon. CutoutSubdesignType.Conformal
CutoutSubdesignType.BBox are surpported.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.cutout_subdesign_expansion = "4mm"
```

Define the distance used for computing the extent polygon. Integer or string can be passed.
For example 0.001 is in meter so here 1mm. You can also pass the string "1mm" for the same result.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.cutout_subdesign_round_corner = True
```

Boolean to allow using rounded corner for the cutout extent or not.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.use_default_cutout = False
```

When True use the native edb API command to process the cutout. Using False uses
the Pyaedt one which improves the cutout speed.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.generate_solder_balls = True
```

Boolean to activate the solder ball generation on components. When HFSS solver is selected in combination with this
parameter, coaxial ports will be created on solder balls for pins belonging to selected signal nets. If Siwave
solver is selected this parameter will be ignored.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.use_default_coax_port_radial_extension = True
```

When `True` the default coaxial extent is used for the ports (only for HFSS).
When the design is having dense solder balls close to each other (like typically package design), the default value
might be too large and cause port overlapping, then solver failure. To prevent this issue set this parameter to
`False` will use a smaller value.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.output_aedb = r"C:    emp\my_edb.aedb"
```

Specify the output edb file after building the project. The parameter must be the complete file path.
leaving this parameter blank will oervwritte the current open edb.

```pycon
>>>  sim_setup.dielectric_extent = 0.01
```

Gives the dielectric extent after cutout, keeping default value is advised unless for
very specific application.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.airbox_horizontal_extent = "5mm"
```

Provide the air box horizonzal extent values. Unitless float value will be
treated as ratio but string value like "5mm" is also supported.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.airbox_negative_vertical_extent = "5mm"
```

Provide the air box negative vertical extent values. Unitless float value will be
treated as ratio but string value like "5mm" is also supported.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.airbox_positive_vertical_extent = "5mm"
```

Provide the air box positive vertical extent values. Unitless float value will be
treated as ratio but string value like "5mm" is also supported.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.use_radiation_boundary = True
```

When `True` use radiation airbox boundary condition and perfect metal box when
set to `False`. Default value is `True`, using enclosed metal box will greatly change simulation results.
Setting this parameter as `False` must be used cautiously.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.do_cutout_subdesign = True
```

`True` activates the cutout with associated parameters. Setting `False` will
keep the entire layout.
Setting to `False` can impact the simulation run time or even memory failure if HFSS solver is used.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.do_pin_group = False
```

When circuit ports are used, setting to `True` will force to create pin groups on
components having pins belonging to same net. Setting to `False` will generate port on each signal pin with
taking the closest reference pin. The last configuration is more often used when users are creating ports on PDN
(Power delivery Network) and want to connect all pins individually.

```pycon
>>> from dotnet.generic.constants import SweepType
>>> sim_setup.sweep_type = SweepType.Linear
```

Specify the frequency sweep type, Linear or Log sweep can be defined.

SimulationCOnfiguration also inherit from SimulationConfigurationAc class for High frequency settings.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.start_freq = "OHz"
```

Define the start frequency from the sweep.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.stop_freq = "40GHz"
```

Define the stop frequency from the sweep.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.step_freq = "10MHz"
```

Define the step frequency from the sweep.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.decade_count = 100
```

Used when log sweep is defined and specify the number of points per decade.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.enforce_causality = True
```

Activate the option `Enforce Causality` for the solver, recommended for signal integrity application

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.enforce_passivity = True
```

Activate the option `Enforce Passivity` for the solver, recommended for signal integrity application

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.do_lambda_refinement = True
```

Activate the lambda refinement for the initial mesh (only for HFSS), default value is `True`. Keeping this
activated is highly recommended.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.use_q3d_for_dc = False
```

Enable when `True` the Q3D DC point computation. Only needed when very high accuracy is required for DC point.
Can eventually cause extra computation time.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.sweep_name = "Test_sweep"
```

Define the frequency sweep name.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.mesh_freq = "10GHz"
```

Define the frequency used for adaptive meshing (available for both HFSS and SIwave).

```pycon
>>> from dotnet.generic.constants import RadiationBoxType
>>> sim_setup.radiation_box = RadiationBoxType.ConvexHull
```

Defined the radiation box type, Conformal, Bounding box and ConvexHull are supported (HFSS only).

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.max_num_passes= 30
```

Default value is 30, specify the maximum number of adaptive passes (only HFSS). Reasonable high value is recommended
to force the solver reaching the convergence criteria.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.max_mag_delta_s = 0.02
```

Define the convergence criteria

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.min_num_passes = 2
```

specify the minimum number of consecutive coberged passes. Setting to 2 is a good practice to avoid converging on
local minima.

```pycon
>>> from dotnet.generic.constants import BasisOrder
>>> sim_setup.basis_order =  BasisOrder.Single
```

Select the order basis (HFSS only), Zero, Single, Double and Mixed are supported. For Signal integrity Single or
Mixed should be used.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.minimum_void_surface = 0
```

Only for Siwave, specify the minimum void surface to be meshed. Void with lower surface value will be ignored by
meshing.

SimulationConfiguration also inherits from SimulationDc class to handle DC simulation projects.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_compute_inductance = True
```

`True` activate the DC loop inductance computation (Siwave only), `False` is deactivated.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_slide_position = 1
```

The provided value must be between 0 and 2 and correspond ti the SIwave DC slide position in GUI.
0 : coarse
1 : medium accuracy
2 : high accuracy

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_plot_jv = True
```

`True` activate the current / voltage plot with Siwave DC solver, `False` deactivate.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_error_energy = 0.02
```

Fix the DC error convergence criteria. In this example 2% is defined.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_max_num_pass = 6
```

Provide the maximum number of passes during Siwave DC adaptive meshing.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_min_num_pass = 1
```

Provide the minimum number of passes during Siwave DC adaptive meshing.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_mesh_bondwires = True
```

`True` bondwires are meshed, `False` bond wires are ignored during meshing.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_num_bondwire_sides = 8
```

Gives the number of facets wirebonds are discretized.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_refine_vias = True
```

`True` meshing refinement on nondwires activated during meshing process. Deactivated when set to `False`.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_report_show_Active_devices = True
```

Activate when `True` the components showing in the DC report.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_export_thermal_data = True
```

`True` thermal data are exported for Icepak simulation.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_full_report_path = r"C:    emp\my_report.html"
```

Provides the file path for the DC report.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_icepak_temp_file = r"C:    emp\my_file"
```

Provides icepak temporary files location.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_import_thermal_data = False
```

Import DC thermal data when `` True` ``

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_per_pin_res_path = r"C:    emp\dc_pin_res_file"
Provides the resistance per pin file path.
```

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_per_pin_use_pin_format = True
```

When `True` activate the pin format.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_use_loop_res_for_per_pin = True
```

Activate the loop resistance usage per pin when `True`

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.dc_via_report_path = 'C:\temp\via_report_file'
```

Define the via report path file.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.add_current_source(name="test_isrc",
>>>                                 current_value=1.2,
>>>                                 phase_value=0.0,
>>>                                 impedance=5e7,
>>>                                 positive_node_component="comp1",
>>>                                 positive_node_net="net1",
>>>                                 negative_node_component="comp2",
>>>                                 negative_node_net="net2"
>>>                             )
```

Define a current source.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.add_dc_ground_source_term(source_name="test_isrc", node_to_ground=1)
```

Define the pin from a source which has to be set to reference for DC simulation.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.add_voltage_source(name="test_vsrc",
>>>                                 current_value=1.33,
>>>                                 phase_value=0.0,
>>>                                 impedance=1e-6,
>>>                                 positive_node_component="comp1",
>>>                                 positive_node_net="net1",
>>>                                 negative_node_component="comp2",
>>>                                 negative_node_net="net2"
>>>                             )
```

Define a voltage source.

```pycon
>>> sim_setup.add_dc_ground_source_term(source_name="test_vsrc", node_to_ground=1)
```

Define the pin from a source which has to be set to reference for DC simulation.

```pycon
>>> edb.build_simulation_project(sim_setup)
```

Will build and save your project.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.hfss.EdbHfss._create_edge_terminal
documentation:
Create an edge terminal.

# Parameters

prim_id

: Primitive ID.

point_on_edge

: Coordinate of the point to define the edge terminal.
  The point must be on the target edge but not on the two
  ends of the edge.

terminal_name

: Name of the terminal. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

is_ref

: Whether it is a reference terminal. The default is `False`.

# Returns

Edb.Cell.Terminal.EdgeTerminal

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.hfss.EdbHfss.create_circuit_port_on_pin
documentation:
Create Circuit Port on Pin.

# Parameters

pos_pin

: Edb Pin

neg_pin

: Edb Pin

impedance

: Port Impedance

port_name

: Port Name

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", "project name", "release version")
>>> pins =edbapp.components.get_pin_from_component("U2A5")
>>> edbapp.hfss.create_circuit_port_on_pin(pins[0], pins[1],50,"port_name")
```

# Returns

str

: Port Name.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.hfss.EdbHfss.create_voltage_source_on_pin

: Negative Pin.

```pycon
>>> pins =edbapp.components.get_pin_from_component("U2A5")
>>> edbapp.hfss.create_voltage_source_on_pin(pins[0], pins[1],50,"source_name")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.hfss.EdbHfss.create_circuit_port_on_net
documentation:
Create a circuit port on a NET.
It groups all pins belonging to the specified net and then applies the port on PinGroups.

# Parameters

positive_component_name

: Name of the positive component.

positive_net_name

: Name of the positive net.

negative_component_name

: Name of the negative component. The default is `None`, in which case the name of
  the positive net is assigned.

negative_net_name

: Name of the negative net name. The default is `"GND"`.

impedance_value

: Port impedance value. The default is `50`.

port_name

: Name of the port. The default is `""`.

# Returns

str

: The name of the port.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", "project name", "release version")
>>> edbapp.hfss.create_circuit_port_on_net("U2A5", "V1P5_S3", "U2A5", "GND", 50, "port_name")
```
