methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.hfss.EdbHfss.create_coax_port_on_component
documentation:
Create a coaxial port on a component or component list on a net or net list.

: The name of the new coaxial port is automatically assigned.

# Parameters

documentation:
Create a differential wave port.

# Parameters

positive_primitive_id

: Primitive ID of the positive terminal.

positive_points_on_edge

: Coordinate of the point to define the edge terminal.
  The point must be close to the target edge but not on the two
  ends of the edge.

negative_primitive_id

: Primitive ID of the negative terminal.

negative_points_on_edge

: Coordinate of the point to define the edge terminal.
  The point must be close to the target edge but not on the two
  ends of the edge.

port_name

: Name of the port. The default is `None`.

horizontal_extent_factor

: Horizontal extent factor. The default value is `5`.

vertical_extent_factor

: Vertical extent factor. The default value is `3`.

pec_launch_width

: Launch Width of PEC. The default value is `"0.01mm"`.

# Returns

tuple

: The tuple contains: (port_name, pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.sources.ExcitationDifferential).

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb.hfss.create_differential_wave_port(0, ["-50mm", "-0mm"], 1, ["-50mm", "-0.2mm"])
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.hfss.EdbHfss.create_edge_port_on_polygon
documentation:
Create lumped port between two edges from two different polygons. Can also create a vertical port when
the reference layer name is only provided. When a port is created between two edge from two polygons which don't
belong to the same layer, a circuit port will be automatically created instead of lumped. To enforce the circuit
port instead of lumped,use the boolean force_circuit_port.

# Parameters

polygon

: Edb.Cell.Primitive.Polygon object.

reference_polygon

: Edb.Cell.Primitive.Polygon object.

terminal_point : The coordinate of the point to define the edge terminal of the port. This point must be
located on the edge of the polygon where the port has to be placed. For instance taking the middle point
of an edge is a good practice but any point of the edge should be valid. Taking a corner might cause unwanted
port location.

> list[float, float] with values provided in meter.

reference_point

: list[float, float] with values provided in meter.

reference_layer

: str the layer name.

port_name

: str.

port_impedance

: float, impedance value.

force_circuit_port ; used to force circuit port creation instead of lumped. Works for vertical and coplanar
ports.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb_path = path_to_edb
>>> edb = Edb(edb_path)
>>> poly_list = [poly for poly in list(edb.layout.primitives) if poly.GetPrimitiveType() == 2]
>>> port_poly = [poly for poly in poly_list if poly.GetId() == 17][0]
>>> ref_poly = [poly for poly in poly_list if poly.GetId() == 19][0]
>>> port_location = [-65e-3, -13e-3]
>>> ref_location = [-63e-3, -13e-3]
>>> edb.hfss.create_edge_port_on_polygon(polygon=port_poly, reference_polygon=ref_poly,
>>> terminal_point=port_location, reference_point=ref_location)
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.hfss.EdbHfss.create_lumped_port_on_net
documentation:
Create an edge port on nets. This command looks for traces and polygons on the
nets and tries to assign vertical lumped port.

# Parameters

nets

: List of nets, str or Edb net.

reference_layer

: Name or Edb layer object.

return_points_only

: Use this boolean when you want to return only the points from the edges and not creating ports. Default
  value is `False`.

digit_resolution

: The number of digits carried for the edge location accuracy. The default value is `6`.

at_bounding_box
: When `True` will keep the edges from traces at the layout bounding box location. This is recommended when

  : a cutout has been performed before and lumped ports have to be created on ending traces. Default value is
    `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.modeler.Modeler.get_polygon_points
documentation:
Retrieve polygon points.

:::{note}
For arcs, one point is returned.
:::

# Parameters

polygon :

: class: `dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.primitives_data.Primitive`

# Returns

list
: List of tuples. Each tuple provides x, y point coordinate. If the length of two consecutives tuples

  : from the list equals 2, a segment is defined. The first tuple defines the starting point while the second
    tuple the ending one. If the length of one tuple equals one, that means a polyline is defined and the value
    is giving the arc height. Therefore to polyline is defined as starting point for the tuple
    before in the list, the current one the arc height and the tuple after the polyline ending point.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> poly = edb_core.modeler.get_polygons_by_layer("GND")
>>> points  = edb_core.modeler.get_polygon_points(poly[0])
```

  \- [x, y, rotation, xc, yc] for an arc given a point, rotation and center.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.net_class.EdbExtendedNets.auto_identify_signal
documentation:
Get extended signal net and associated components.

# Parameters

resistor_below

: Threshold for the resistor value. Search the extended net across resistors that
  have a value lower than the threshold.

inductor_below

: Threshold for the inductor value. Search the extended net across inductances
  that have a value lower than the threshold.

capacitor_above

: Threshold for the capacitor value. Search the extended net across capacitors
  that have a value higher than the threshold.

exception_list

: List of components to bypass when performing threshold checks. Components
  in the list are considered as serial components. The default is `None`.

# Returns

list

: List of all extended nets.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> app = Edb()
>>> app.extended_nets.auto_identify_signal()
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.get_component_placement_vector
documentation:
Get the placement vector between 2 components.

# Parameters

mounted_component

: Mounted component name.

hosting_component

: Hosting component name.

mounted_component_pin1

: Mounted component Pin 1 name.

mounted_component_pin2

: Mounted component Pin 2 name.

hosting_component_pin1

: Hosted component Pin 1 name.

hosting_component_pin2

: Hosted component Pin 2 name.

flipped

: Either if the mounted component will be flipped or not.

# Returns

tuple

: Tuple of Vector offset, rotation and solder height.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb1 = Edb(edbpath=targetfile1,  edbversion="2021.2")
>>> hosting_cmp = edb1.components.get_component_by_name("U100")
>>> mounted_cmp = edb2.components.get_component_by_name("BGA")
>>> vector, rotation, solder_ball_height = edb1.components.get_component_placement_vector(
...                                             mounted_component=mounted_cmp,
...                                             hosting_component=hosting_cmp,
...                                             mounted_component_pin1="A12",
...                                             mounted_component_pin2="A14",
...                                             hosting_component_pin1="A12",
...                                             hosting_component_pin2="A14")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.create_port_on_pins
documentation:
Create circuit port between pins and reference ones.

# Parameters

refdes

: str or EDBComponent object.

pins : pin name where the terminal has to be created. Single pin or several ones can be provided.If several
pins are provided a pin group will is created. Pin names can be the EDB name or the EDBPadstackInstance one.
For instance the pin called `Pin1` located on component `U1`, `U1-Pin1` or `Pin1` can be provided and
will be handled.

> str, [str], EDBPadstackInstance, [EDBPadstackInstance]

reference_pins : reference pin name used for terminal reference. Single pin or several ones can be provided.
If several pins are provided a pin group will is created. Pin names can be the EDB name or the
EDBPadstackInstance one. For instance the pin called `Pin1` located on component `U1`, `U1-Pin1`
or `Pin1` can be provided and will be handled.

> str, [str], EDBPadstackInstance, [EDBPadstackInstance]

impedance

: str, float

port_name

: Port name. The default is `None`, in which case a name is automatically assigned.

pec_boundary : bool, optional
Whether to define the PEC boundary, The default is `False`. If set to `True`,
a perfect short is created between the pin and impedance is ignored. This
parameter is only supported on a port created between two pins, such as
when there is no pin group.
pingroup_on_single_pin : bool

> If `True` force using pingroup definition on single pin to have the port created at the pad center. If
> `False` the port is created at the pad edge. Default value is `False`.

# Returns

EDB terminal created, or False if failed to create.

Example:
\>>> from pyedb import Edb
\>>> edb = Edb(path_to_edb_file)
\>>> pin = "AJ6"
\>>> ref_pins = ["AM7", "AM4"]
Or to take all reference pins
\>>> ref_pins = \[pin for pin in list(edb.components["U2A5"].pins.values()) if pin.net_name == "GND"\]
\>>> edb.components.create_port_on_pins(refdes="U2A5", pins=pin, reference_pins=ref_pins)
\>>> edb.save_edb()
\>>> edb.close_edb()

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.create_port_on_component
documentation:
Create ports on a component.

# Parameters

component

: EDB component or str component name.

net_list

: List of nets where ports must be created on the component.
  If the net is not part of the component, this parameter is skipped.

port_type

: Type of port to create. `CoaxPort` generates solder balls.
  `CircuitPort` generates circuit ports on pins belonging to the net list.

do_pingroup

: True activate pingroup during port creation (only used with combination of CircPort),
  False will take the closest reference pin and generate one port per signal pin.

refnet

: list of the reference net.

port_name

: Port name for overwriting the default port-naming convention,
  which is `[component][net][pin]`. The port name must be unique.
  If a port with the specified name already exists, the
  default naming convention is used so that port creation does
  not fail.

solder_balls_height

: Solder balls height used for the component. When provided default value is overwritten and must be
  provided in meter.

solder_balls_size

: Solder balls diameter. When provided auto evaluation based on padstack size will be disabled.

solder_balls_mid_size

: Solder balls mid-diameter. When provided if value is different than solder balls size, spheroid shape will
  be switched.

extend_reference_pins_outside_component

: When no reference pins are found on the component extend the pins search with taking the closest one. If
  `do_pingroup` is `True` will be set to `False`. Default value is `False`.

# Returns

double, bool

: Salder ball height vale, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder")
>>> net_list = ["M_DQ<1>", "M_DQ<2>", "M_DQ<3>", "M_DQ<4>", "M_DQ<5>"]
>>> edbapp.components.create_port_on_component(cmp="U2A5", net_list=net_list,
>>> port_type=SourceType.CoaxPort, do_pingroup=False, refnet="GND")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.replace_rlc_by_gap_boundaries
documentation:
Replace RLC component by RLC gap boundaries. These boundary types are compatible with 3D modeler export.
Only 2 pins RLC components are supported in this command.

# Parameters

component

: Reference designator of the RLC component.

# Returns

bool
`True` when succeed, `False` if it failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb(edb_file)
>>>  for refdes, cmp in edb.components.capacitors.items():
>>>     edb.components.replace_rlc_by_gap_boundaries(refdes)
>>> edb.save_edb()
>>> edb.close_edb()
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.deactivate_rlc_component
documentation:
Deactivate RLC component with a possibility to convert it to a circuit port.

# Parameters

component

: Reference designator of the RLC component.

create_circuit_port

: Whether to replace the deactivated RLC component with a circuit port. The default
  is `False`.

pec_boundary

: Whether to define the PEC boundary, The default is `False`. If set to `True`,
  a perfect short is created between the pin and impedance is ignored. This
  parameter is only supported on a port created between two pins, such as
  when there is no pin group.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb_file = r'C:\my_edb_file.aedb'
>>> edb = Edb(edb_file)
>>> for cmp in list(edb.components.instances.keys()):
>>>     edb.components.deactivate_rlc_component(component=cmp, create_circuit_port=False)
>>> edb.save_edb()
>>> edb.close_edb()
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.add_port_on_rlc_component
documentation:
Deactivate RLC component and replace it with a circuit port.
The circuit port supports only two-pin components.

# Parameters

component

: Reference designator of the RLC component.

circuit_ports

: `True` will replace RLC component by circuit ports, `False` gap ports compatible with HFSS 3D modeler
  export.

pec_boundary

: Whether to define the PEC boundary, The default is `False`. If set to `True`,
  a perfect short is created between the pin and impedance is ignored. This
  parameter is only supported on a port created between two pins, such as
  when there is no pin group.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.add_rlc_boundary
documentation:
Add RLC gap boundary on component and replace it with a circuit port.
The circuit port supports only 2-pin components.

# Parameters

component

: Reference designator of the RLC component.

circuit_type

: When `True` circuit type are defined, if `False` gap type will be used instead (compatible with HFSS 3D
  modeler). Default value is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components._create_pin_group_terminal
documentation:
Creates an EDB pin group terminal from a given EDB pin group.

Parameters
----------
pingroup : Edb pin group.

isref : bool
Specify if this terminal a reference terminal.

term_name : Terminal name (Optional). If not provided default name is Component name, Pin name, Net name.
    str.

term_type: Type of terminal, gap, circuit or auto.
str.
Returns
-------
Edb pin group terminal.

documentation:
Test the component placement layer.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.create_rlc_component
documentation:
Create physical Rlc component.

# Parameters

pins

: List of EDB pins, length must be 2, since only 2 pins component are currently supported.
  It can be an `dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.padstacks_data.EDBPadstackInstance` object or
  an Edb Padstack Instance object.

component_name

: Component definition name.

r_value

: Resistor value.

c_value

: Capacitance value.

l_value

: Inductor value.

is_parallel

: Using parallel model when `True`, series when `False`.

# Returns

Component

: Created EDB component.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.create
documentation:
Create a component from pins.

# Parameters

pins

: List of EDB core pins.

component_name

: Name of the reference designator for the component.

placement_layer

: Name of the layer used for placing the component.

component_part_name

: Part name of the component.

is_rlc

: Whether if the new component will be an RLC or not.

r_value

: Resistor value.

c_value

: Capacitance value.

l_value

: Inductor value.

is_parallel

: Using parallel model when `True`, series when `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder")
>>> pins = edbapp.components.get_pin_from_component("A1")
>>> edbapp.components.create(pins, "A1New")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.create_component_from_pins
documentation:
Create a component from pins.

:::{deprecated} 0.6.62 Use :func:`create` method instead.
:::

pins

: List of EDB core pins.

component_name

: Name of the reference designator for the component.

placement_layer

: Name of the layer used for placing the component.

component_part_name

: Part name of the component. It's created a new definition if doesn't exists.

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder")
>>> pins = edbapp.components.get_pin_from_component("A1")
>>> edbapp.components.create(pins, "A1New")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.set_component_model
documentation:
Assign a Spice or Touchstone model to a component.

componentname

: Name of the component.

model_type

: Type of the model. Options are `"Spice"` and
  `"Touchstone"`. The default is `"Spice"`.

modelpath

: Full path to the model file. The default is `None`.

modelname

: Name of the model. The default is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder")
>>> edbapp.components.set_component_model("A1", model_type="Spice",
...                                            modelpath="pathtospfile",
...                                            modelname="spicemodelname")
```

Create a pin group on a component.

  a default name is assigned as follows: `[component Name] [NetName]`.

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder")
```

documentation:

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder")

>>> print(list_of_deleted_rlcs)
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.set_solder_ball
documentation:
Set cylindrical solder balls on a given component.

# Parameters

component

: Name of the discrete component.

sball_diam

: Diameter of the solder ball.

sball_height

: Height of the solder ball.

shape

: Shape of solder ball. Options are `"Cylinder"`,
  `"Spheroid"`. The default is `"Cylinder"`.

sball_mid_diam

: Mid diameter of the solder ball.

chip_orientation

: Give the chip orientation, `"chip_down"` or `"chip_up"`. Default is `"chip_down"`. Only applicable on
  IC model.

auto_reference_size

: Whether to automatically set reference size.

reference_size_x

: X size of the reference. Applicable when auto_reference_size is False.

reference_size_y

: Y size of the reference. Applicable when auto_reference_size is False.

reference_height

: Height of the reference. Applicable when auto_reference_size is False.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder")
>>> edbapp.components.set_solder_ball("A1")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.set_component_rlc
documentation:
Update values for an RLC component.

# Parameters

componentname :

: Name of the RLC component.

res_value

: Resistance value. The default is `None`.

ind_value

: Inductor value. The default is `None`.

cap_value

: Capacitor value. The default is `None`.

isparallel

: Whether the RLC component is parallel. The default is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder")
>>> edbapp.components.set_component_rlc(
...     "R1", res_value=50, ind_value=1e-9, cap_value=1e-12, isparallel=False
... )
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.update_rlc_from_bom
documentation:
Update the EDC core component values (RLCs) with values coming from a BOM file.

# Parameters

bom_file

: Full path to the BOM file, which is a delimited text file.
  Header values needed inside the BOM reader must
  be explicitly set if different from the defaults.

delimiter

: Value to use for the delimiter. The default is `";"`.

valuefield

: Field header containing the value of the component. The default is `"Func des"`.
  The value for this parameter must being with the value of the component
  followed by a space and then the rest of the value. For example, `"22pF"`.

comptype

: Field header containing the type of component. The default is `"Prod name"`. For
  example, you might enter `"Inductor"`.

refdes

: Field header containing the reference designator of the component. The default is
  `"Pos / Place"`. For example, you might enter `"C100"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the file contains the header and it is correctly parsed. `True` is
  returned even if no values are assigned.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.import_bom
documentation:
Load external BOM file.

# Parameters

bom_file

: Full path to the BOM file, which is a delimited text file.

delimiter

: Value to use for the delimiter. The default is `","`.

refdes_col

: Column index of reference designator. The default is `"0"`.

part_name_col

: Column index of part name. The default is `"1"`. Set to `None` if
  the column does not exist.

comp_type_col

: Column index of component type. The default is `"2"`.

value_col

: Column index of value. The default is `"3"`. Set to `None`
  if the column does not exist.

# Returns

bool

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.components.Components.export_bom
documentation:
Export Bom file from layout.

# Parameters

bom_file

: Full path to the BOM file, which is a delimited text file.

delimiter

: Value to use for the delimiter. The default is `","`.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.siwave.EdbSiwave.create_circuit_port_on_net
documentation:
Create a circuit port on a NET.

It groups all pins belonging to the specified net and then applies the port on PinGroups.

# Parameters

positive_component_name

: Name of the positive component.

positive_net_name

: Name of the positive net.

negative_component_name

: Name of the negative component. The default is `None`, in which case the name of
  the positive net is assigned.

negative_net_name

: Name of the negative net name. The default is `None` which will look for GND Nets.

impedance_value

: Port impedance value. The default is `50`.

port_name

: Name of the port. The default is `""`.

# Returns

str

: The name of the port.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", "project name", "release version")
>>> edbapp.siwave.create_circuit_port_on_net("U2A5", "V1P5_S3", "U2A5", "GND", 50, "port_name")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.siwave.EdbSiwave.create_voltage_source_on_net
documentation:
Create a voltage source.

# Parameters

positive_component_name

: Name of the positive component.

positive_net_name

: Name of the positive net.

negative_component_name

: Name of the negative component. The default is `None`, in which case the name of
  the positive net is assigned.

negative_net_name

: Name of the negative net name. The default is `None` which will look for GND Nets.

voltage_value

: Value for the voltage. The default is `3.3`.

phase_value

: Value for the phase. The default is `0`.

source_name

: Name of the source. The default is `""`.

# Returns

str

: The name of the source.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", "project name", "release version")
>>> edb.siwave.create_voltage_source_on_net("U2A5","V1P5_S3","U2A5","GND",3.3,0,"source_name")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.siwave.EdbSiwave.create_current_source_on_net
documentation:
Create a current source.

# Parameters

positive_component_name

: Name of the positive component.

positive_net_name

: Name of the positive net.

negative_component_name

: Name of the negative component. The default is `None`, in which case the name of
  the positive net is assigned.

negative_net_name

: Name of the negative net name. The default is `None` which will look for GND Nets.

current_value

: Value for the current. The default is `0.1`.

phase_value

: Value for the phase. The default is `0`.

source_name

: Name of the source. The default is `""`.

# Returns

str

: The name of the source.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", "project name", "release version")
>>> edb.siwave.create_current_source_on_net("U2A5", "V1P5_S3", "U2A5", "GND", 0.1, 0, "source_name")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.siwave.EdbSiwave.create_dc_terminal
documentation:
Create a dc terminal.

# Parameters

component_name

: Name of the positive component.

net_name

: Name of the positive net.

source_name

: Name of the source. The default is `""`.

# Returns

str

: The name of the source.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", "project name", "release version")
>>> edb.siwave.create_dc_terminal("U2A5", "V1P5_S3", "source_name")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.siwave.EdbSiwave.create_exec_file
documentation:
Create an executable file.

# Parameters

add_dc

: Whether to add the DC option in the EXE file. The default is `False`.

add_ac

: Whether to add the AC option in the EXE file. The default is
  `False`.

add_syz

: Whether to add the SYZ option in the EXE file

export_touchstone

: Add the Touchstone file export option in the EXE file.
  The default is `False`.

touchstone_file_path

: File path for the Touchstone file. The default is `""`. When no path is
  specified and `export_touchstone=True`, the path for the project is
  used.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.siwave.EdbSiwave.add_siwave_syz_analysis
documentation:
Add a SIwave AC analysis to EDB.

# Parameters

accuracy_level

: Level of accuracy of SI slider. The default is `1`.

decade_count

: The default is `10`. The value for this parameter is used for these sweep types:
  linear count and decade count.
  This parameter is alternative to `step_freq`, which is used for a linear scale sweep.

sweeptype

: Type of the sweep. The default is `1`. Options are:

  - `0`: linear count
  - `1`: linear scale
  - `2`: loc scale

start_freq

: Starting frequency. The default is `1`.

stop_freq

: Stopping frequency. The default is `1e9`.

step_freq

: Frequency size of the step. The default is `1e6`.

discrete_sweep

: Whether the sweep is discrete. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.siwave_simulation_setup_data.SiwaveSYZSimulationSetup`

: Setup object class.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.siwave.EdbSiwave.add_siwave_dc_analysis
documentation:
Add a Siwave DC analysis in EDB.

If a setup is present, it is deleted and replaced with
actual settings.

:::{note}
Source Reference to Ground settings works only from 2021.2
:::

# Parameters

name

: Setup name.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.siwave_simulation_setup_data.SiwaveDCSimulationSetup`

: Setup object class.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb("pathtoaedb", edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb.siwave.add_siwave_ac_analysis()
>>> edb.siwave.add_siwave_dc_analysis2("my_setup")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.add_layer
documentation:
Insert a layer into stackup.

# Parameters

layer_name

: Name of the layer.

base_layer

: Name of the base layer.

method

: Where to insert the new layer. The default is `"add_on_top"`. Options are `"add_on_top"`,
  `"add_on_bottom"`, `"insert_above"`, `"insert_below"`, `"add_at_elevation"`,.

layer_type
: Type of layer. The default is `"signal"`. Options are `"signal"`, `"dielectric"`, `"conducting"`,

  : `"air_lines"`, `"error"`, `"symbol"`, `"measure"`, `"assembly"`, `"silkscreen"`,
    `"solder_mask"`, `"solder_paste"`, `"glue"`, `"wirebond"`, `"hfss_region"`, `"user"`.

material

: Material of the layer.

fillMaterial

: Fill material of the layer.

thickness

: Thickness of the layer.

etch_factor

: Etch factor of the layer.

is_negative

: Whether the layer is negative.

enable_roughness

: Whether roughness is enabled.

elevation

: Elevation of new layer. Only valid for Overlapping Stackup.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.layer_data.LayerEdbClass`

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.export
documentation:
Export stackup definition to a CSV or JSON file.

# Parameters

fpath

: File path to csv or json file.

file_format

: Format of the file to export. The default is `"csv"`. Options are `"csv"`, `"xlsx"`,
  `"json"`.

include_material_with_layer

: Whether to include the material definition inside layer ones. This parameter is only used
  when a JSON file is exported. The default is `False`, which keeps the material definition
  section in the JSON file. If `True`, the material definition is included inside the layer ones.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb()
>>> edb.stackup.export("stackup.xml")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.export_stackup
documentation:
Export stackup definition to a CSV or JSON file.

:::{deprecated} 0.6.61 Use :func:`export` instead.
:::

# Parameters

fpath

: File path to CSV or JSON file.

file_format

: Format of the file to export. The default is `"csv"`. Options are `"csv"`, `"xlsx"`
  and `"json"`.

include_material_with_layer

: Whether to include the material definition inside layer objects. This parameter is only used
  when a JSON file is exported. The default is `False`, which keeps the material definition
  section in the JSON file. If `True`, the material definition is included inside the layer ones.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb()
>>> edb.stackup.export_stackup("stackup.xml")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.place_in_layout
documentation:
Place current Cell into another cell using layer placement method.
Flip the current layer stackup of a layout if requested. Transform parameters currently not supported.

# Parameters

edb

: Cell on which to place the current layout. If None the Cell will be applied on an empty new Cell.

angle

: The rotation angle applied on the design.

offset_x

: The x offset value.

offset_y

: The y offset value.

flipped_stackup

: Either if the current layout is inverted.
  If `True` and place_on_top is `True` the stackup will be flipped before the merge.

place_on_top

: Either if place the current layout on Top or Bottom of destination Layout.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when succeed `False` if not.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb1 = Edb(edbpath=targetfile1,  edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb2 = Edb(edbpath=targetfile2, edbversion="2021.2")
```

```pycon
>>> hosting_cmp = edb1.components.get_component_by_name("U100")
>>> mounted_cmp = edb2.components.get_component_by_name("BGA")
```

```pycon
>>> vector, rotation, solder_ball_height = edb1.components.get_component_placement_vector(
...                                                     mounted_component=mounted_cmp,
...                                                     hosting_component=hosting_cmp,
...                                                     mounted_component_pin1="A12",
...                                                     mounted_component_pin2="A14",
...                                                     hosting_component_pin1="A12",
...                                                     hosting_component_pin2="A14")
>>> edb2.stackup.place_in_layout(edb1.active_cell, angle=0.0, offset_x=vector[0],
...                              offset_y=vector[1], flipped_stackup=False, place_on_top=True,
...                              )
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.place_in_layout_3d_placement
documentation:
Place current Cell into another cell using 3d placement method.
Flip the current layer stackup of a layout if requested. Transform parameters currently not supported.

# Parameters

edb

: Cell on which to place the current layout. If None the Cell will be applied on an empty new Cell.

angle

: The rotation angle applied on the design.

offset_x

: The x offset value.

offset_y

: The y offset value.

flipped_stackup

: Either if the current layout is inverted.
  If `True` and place_on_top is `True` the stackup will be flipped before the merge.

place_on_top

: Either if place the current layout on Top or Bottom of destination Layout.

solder_height

: Solder Ball or Bumps eight.
  This value will be added to the elevation to align the two layouts.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when succeed `False` if not.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb1 = Edb(edbpath=targetfile1,  edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb2 = Edb(edbpath=targetfile2, edbversion="2021.2")
>>> hosting_cmp = edb1.components.get_component_by_name("U100")
>>> mounted_cmp = edb2.components.get_component_by_name("BGA")
>>> edb2.stackup.place_in_layout(edb1.active_cell, angle=0.0, offset_x="1mm",
...                                   offset_y="2mm", flipped_stackup=False, place_on_top=True,
...                                   )
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.place_instance
documentation:
Place current Cell into another cell using 3d placement method.
Flip the current layer stackup of a layout if requested. Transform parameters currently not supported.

# Parameters

component_edb

: Cell to place in the current layout.

angle

: The rotation angle applied on the design.

offset_x

: The x offset value.
  The default value is `0.0`.

offset_y

: The y offset value.
  The default value is `0.0`.

offset_z

: The z offset value. (i.e. elevation offset for placement relative to the top layer conductor).
  The default value is `0.0`, which places the cell layout on top of the top conductor
  layer of the target EDB.

flipped_stackup

: Either if the current layout is inverted.
  If `True` and place_on_top is `True` the stackup will be flipped before the merge.

place_on_top

: Either if place the component_edb layout on Top or Bottom of destination Layout.

solder_height

: Solder Ball or Bumps eight.
  This value will be added to the elevation to align the two layouts.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when succeed `False` if not.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb1 = Edb(edbpath=targetfile1,  edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb2 = Edb(edbpath=targetfile2, edbversion="2021.2")
>>> hosting_cmp = edb1.components.get_component_by_name("U100")
>>> mounted_cmp = edb2.components.get_component_by_name("BGA")
>>> edb1.stackup.place_instance(edb2, angle=0.0, offset_x="1mm",
...                                   offset_y="2mm", flipped_stackup=False, place_on_top=True,
...                                   )
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.place_a3dcomp_3d_placement
documentation:
Place a 3D Component into current layout.

: 3D Component ports are not visible via EDB. They will be visible after the EDB has been opened in Ansys
  Electronics Desktop as a project.

# Parameters

a3dcomp_path

: Path to the 3D Component file (\*.a3dcomp) to place.

angle

: Clockwise rotation angle applied to the a3dcomp.

offset_x

: The x offset value.
  The default value is `0.0`.

offset_y

: The y offset value.
  The default value is `0.0`.

offset_z

: The z offset value. (i.e. elevation)
  The default value is `0.0`.

place_on_top

: Whether to place the 3D Component on the top or the bottom of this layout.
  If `False` then the 3D Component will also be flipped over around its X axis.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful and `False` if not.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb1 = Edb(edbpath=targetfile1,  edbversion="2021.2")
>>> a3dcomp_path = "connector.a3dcomp"
>>> edb1.stackup.place_a3dcomp_3d_placement(a3dcomp_path, angle=0.0, offset_x="1mm",
...                                   offset_y="2mm", flipped_stackup=False, place_on_top=True,
...                                   )
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.load
documentation:
Import stackup from a file. The file format can be XML, CSV, or JSON. Valid control file must
have the same number of signal layers. Signals layers can be renamed. Dielectric layers can be
added and deleted.

# Parameters

file_path

: Path to stackup file or dict with stackup details.

rename

: If rename is `False` then layer in layout not found in the stackup file are deleted.
  Otherwise, if the number of layer in the stackup file equals the number of stackup layer
  in the layout, layers are renamed according the file.
  Note that layer order matters, and has to be writtent from top to bottom layer in the file.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb()
>>> edb.stackup.load("stackup.xml")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.stackup.Stackup.plot
documentation:
Plot current stackup and, optionally, overlap padstack definitions.
Plot supports only 'Laminate' and 'Overlapping' stackup types.

# Parameters

save_plot

: If a path is specified the plot will be saved in this location.
  If `save_plot` is provided, the `show` parameter is ignored.

size

: Image size in pixel (width, height). Default value is `(2000, 1500)`

plot_definitions

: List of padstack definitions to plot on the stackup.
  It is supported only for Laminate mode.

first_layer

: First layer to plot from the bottom. Default is `None` to start plotting from bottom.

last_layer

: Last layer to plot from the bottom. Default is `None` to plot up to top layer.

scale_elevation

: The real layer thickness is scaled so that max_thickness = 3 * min_thickness.
  Default is `True`.

show

: Whether to show the plot or not. Default is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.plt`
Manages EDB methods for stackup accessible from `Edb.stackup` property.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.cell.primitive.path.Path.create_edge_port
documentation:
# Parameters

name

: Name of the port.

position

: Position of the port. The default is `"End"`, in which case the port is created at the end of the trace.
  Options are `"Start"` and `"End"`.

port_type
: Type of the port. The default is `"Wave"`, in which case a wave port is created. Options are `"Wave"`

  : and `"Gap"`.

reference_layer

: Name of the references layer. The default is `None`. Only available for gap port.

horizontal_extent_factor

: Horizontal extent factor of the wave port. The default is `5`.

vertical_extent_factor

: Vertical extent factor of the wave port. The default is `3`.

pec_launch_width

: Perfect electrical conductor width of the wave port. The default is `"0.01mm"`.

# Returns

> {class}`dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.sources.ExcitationPorts`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edbapp = pyedb.dotnet.Edb("myproject.aedb")
>>> sig = appedb.modeler.create_trace([[0, 0], ["9mm", 0]], "TOP", "1mm", "SIG", "Flat", "Flat")
>>> sig.create_edge_port("pcb_port", "end", "Wave", None, 8, 8)
```

Manages EDB functionalities for a primitives.
It inherits EDB Object properties.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb(myedb, edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb_prim = edb.modeler.primitives[0]
>>> edb_prim.is_void # Class Property
>>> edb_prim.IsVoid() # EDB Object Property
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.dotnet.primitive.PrimitiveDotNet.expand
documentation:
Expand the polygon shape by an absolute value in all direction.
Offset can be negative for negative expansion.

Parameters
----------
offset : float, optional
    Offset value in meters.
tolerance : float, optional
    Tolerance in meters.
round_corners : bool, optional
    Whether to round corners or not.
    If True, use rounded corners in the expansion otherwise use straight edges (can be degenerate).
maximum_corner_extension : float, optional
    The maximum corner extension (when round corners are not used) at which point the corner is clipped.

: If None scaling is done from polygon center.

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

classes.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.dotnet.primitive.RectangleDotNet

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.dotnet.primitive.RectangleDotNet.create
documentation:
Create a rectangle.

# Parameters

layout

: Layout this rectangle will be in.

layer

: Layer this rectangle will be on.

net

: Net this rectangle will have.

rep_type

: Type that defines given parameters meaning.

param1

: X value of lower left point or center point.

param2

: Y value of lower left point or center point.

param3

: X value of upper right point or width.

param4

: Y value of upper right point or height.

corner_rad

: Corner radius.

rotation

: Rotation.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.dotnet.primitive.RectangleDotNet`

> Rectangle that was created.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.dotnet.primitive.RectangleDotNet.get_parameters
documentation:
Get coordinates parameters.

# Returns

tuple\[

: {class}`RectangleRepresentationType`,
  {class}`Value <ansys.edb.utility.Value>`,
  {class}`Value <ansys.edb.utility.Value>`,
  {class}`Value <ansys.edb.utility.Value>`,
  {class}`Value <ansys.edb.utility.Value>`,
  {class}`Value <ansys.edb.utility.Value>`,
  {class}`Value <ansys.edb.utility.Value>`

\]

> Returns a tuple of the following format:
>
> **(representation_type, parameter1, parameter2, parameter3, parameter4, corner_radius, rotation)**
>
> **representation_type** : Type that defines given parameters meaning.
>
> **parameter1** : X value of lower left point or center point.
>
> **parameter2** : Y value of lower left point or center point.
>
> **parameter3** : X value of upper right point or width.
>
> **parameter4** : Y value of upper right point or height.
>
> **corner_radius** : Corner radius.
>
> **rotation** : Rotation.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.dotnet.primitive.CircleDotNet.expand
documentation:
Expand the polygon shape by an absolute value in all direction.
Offset can be negative for negative expansion.

Parameters
----------
offset : float, optional
    Offset value in meters.
tolerance : float, optional
    Tolerance in meters. Ignored for Circle and Path.
round_corners : bool, optional
    Whether to round corners or not.
    If True, use rounded corners in the expansion otherwise use straight edges (can be degenerate).
     Ignored for Circle and Path.
maximum_corner_extension : float, optional
    The maximum corner extension (when round corners are not used) at which point the corner is clipped.
     Ignored for Circle and Path.
Class representing a circle object.

documentation:
Get the back drill by layer.

# Parameters

from_bottom

: True to get drill type from bottom.

# Returns

tuple\[

: bool,
  {class}`Value <ansys.edb.utility.Value>`,
  {class}`Value <ansys.edb.utility.Value>`

\]

> Returns a tuple of the following format:
>
> **(drill_to_layer, offset, diameter)**
>
> **drill_to_layer** : Layer drills to. If drill from top, drill stops at the upper elevation of the layer. If from bottom, drill stops at the lower elevation of the layer.
>
> **offset** : Layer offset (or depth if layer is empty).
>
> **diameter** : Drilling diameter.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.cell.layout.Layout.expanded_extent
documentation:
Get an expanded polygon for the Nets collection.

# Parameters

nets

: A list of nets.

extent

: Geometry extent type for expansion.

expansion_factor

: Expansion factor for the polygon union. No expansion occurs if the `expansion_factor` is less than or equal to 0.

expansion_unitless

: When unitless, the distance by which the extent expands is the factor multiplied by the longer dimension (X or Y distance) of the expanded object/net.

use_round_corner

: Whether to use round or sharp corners.
  For round corners, this returns a bounding box if its area is within 10% of the rounded expansion's area.

num_increments

: Number of iterations desired to reach the full expansion.

# Returns

{class}`PolygonData <ansys.edb.geometry.PolygonData>`

# Notes

Method returns the expansion of the contour, so any voids within expanded objects are ignored.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.cell.hierarchy.component.EDBComponent.use_s_parameter_model
documentation:
Use S-parameter model on the component.

# Parameters

name: str

: Name of the S-parameter model.

reference_net: str, optional

: Reference net of the model.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edbapp = Edb()
>>>comp_def = edbapp.definitions.components["CAPC3216X180X55ML20T25"]
>>>comp_def.add_n_port_model("c:GRM32_DC0V_25degC_series.s2p", "GRM32_DC0V_25degC_series")
>>>edbapp.components["C200"].use_s_parameter_model("GRM32_DC0V_25degC_series")
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.cell.hierarchy.component.EDBComponent.assign_rlc_model
documentation:
Assign RLC to this component.

# Parameters

res

: Resistance. Default is `None`.

ind

: Inductance. Default is `None`.

cap

: Capacitance. Default is `None`.

is_parallel

: Whether it is a parallel or series RLC component. The default is `False`.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.cell.hierarchy.component.EDBComponent.create_clearance_on_component
documentation:
Create a Clearance on Soldermask layer by drawing a rectangle.

Parameters
----------
extra_soldermask_clearance : float, optional
    Extra Soldermask value in meter to be applied on component bounding box.

Returns
-------
    bool

Manages EDB functionalities for components.

# Parameters

parent

: Components object.

classes.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.layout_validation.LayoutValidation

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.layout_validation.LayoutValidation.dc_shorts
documentation:
Find DC shorts on layout.

# Parameters

net_list

: List of nets.

fix

: If `True`, rename all the nets. (default)
  If `False`, only report dc shorts.

# Returns

List\[List[str, str]\]

: \[[net name, net name]\].

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edb = Edb("edb_file")
>>> dc_shorts = edb.layout_validation.dc_shorts()
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.layout_validation.LayoutValidation.disjoint_nets
documentation:
Find and fix disjoint nets from a given netlist.

# Parameters

net_list

: List of nets on which check disjoints. If `None` is provided then the algorithm will loop on all nets.

keep_only_main_net

: Remove all secondary nets other than principal one (the one with more objects in it). Default is `False`.

clean_disjoints_less_than

: Clean all disjoint nets with area less than specified area in square meters. Default is `0.0` to disable it.

order_by_area

: Whether if the naming order has to be by number of objects (fastest) or area (slowest but more accurate).
  Default is `False`.

keep_disjoint_pins

: Whether if delete disjoints pins not connected to any other primitive or not. Default is `False`.

# Returns

List

: New nets created.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> renamed_nets = edb.layout_validation.disjoint_nets(["GND","Net2"])
```

documentation:
Add a mesh operation to the setup.

# Parameters

net_layer_list

: Dictionary containing nets and layers on which enable Mesh operation. Example `{"A0_N": ["TOP", "PWR"]}`.

name

: Mesh operation name.

max_elements

: Maximum number of elements. Default is `1000`.

max_length

: Maximum length of elements. Default is `1mm`.

restrict_elements

: Whether to restrict number of elements. Default is `True`.

restrict_length

: Whether to restrict length of elements. Default is `True`.

mesh_region

: Mesh region name.

refine_inside

: Whether to refine inside or not. Default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.hfss_simulation_setup_data.LengthMeshOperation`

: Skin Depth. Default is `1um`.

restrict_elements

: Whether to restrict number of elements. Default is `True`.

surface_triangle_length

: Surface Triangle length. Default is `1mm`.

number_of_layers

mesh_region

: Mesh region name.

refine_inside

# Returns

{class}`dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.hfss_simulation_setup_data.LengthMeshOperation`

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.nets.EdbNets.generate_extended_nets
documentation:
Get extended net and associated components.

# Parameters

resistor_below

: Threshold of resistor value. Search extended net across resistors which has value lower than the threshold.

inductor_below

: Threshold of inductor value. Search extended net across inductances which has value lower than the
  threshold.

capacitor_above

: Threshold of capacitor value. Search extended net across capacitors which has value higher than the
  threshold.

exception_list

: List of components to bypass when performing threshold checks. Components
  in the list are considered as serial components. The default is `None`.

include_signal

: Whether to generate extended signal nets. The default is `True`.

include_power

: Whether to generate extended power nets. The default is `True`.

# Returns

list

: List of all extended nets.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> app = Edb()
>>> app.nets.get_extended_nets()
```

: All net name containing the string. Not case-sensitive.

end_with

: All net name ending with the string. Not case-sensitive.

# Returns

object

: Net Object.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.nets.EdbNets.is_net_in_component
documentation:
Check if a net belongs to a component.

# Parameters

component_name

: Name of the component.

net_name

: Name of the net.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the net is found in component pins.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.nets.EdbNets.find_and_fix_disjoint_nets
documentation:
Find and fix disjoint nets from a given netlist.

:::{deprecated} Use new property :func:`edb.layout_validation.disjoint_nets` instead.
:::

# Parameters

net_list

: List of nets on which check disjoints. If `None` is provided then the algorithm will loop on all nets.

keep_only_main_net

: Remove all secondary nets other than principal one (the one with more objects in it). Default is `False`.

clean_disjoints_less_than

: Clean all disjoint nets with area less than specified area in square meters. Default is `0.0` to disable it.

order_by_area

: Whether if the naming order has to be by number of objects (fastest) or area (slowest but more accurate).
  Default is `False`.

# Returns

List

: New nets created.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> renamed_nets = edb_core.nets.find_and_fix_disjoint_nets(["GND","Net2"])
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.nets.EdbNets.merge_nets_polygons
documentation:
Convert paths from net into polygons, evaluate all connected polygons and perform the merge.

# Parameters

net_names_list

: Net name of list of net name.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

Manages EDB methods for nets management accessible from `Edb.nets` property.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb_nets = edbapp.nets
```

documentation:
Add frequency sweep.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the frequency sweep. The default is `None`.

frequency_sweep

: List of frequency points. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.simulation_setup_data.EdbFrequencySweep`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> setup1 = edbapp.create_siwave_syz_setup("setup1")
>>> setup1.add_frequency_sweep(frequency_sweep=[
...     ["linear count", "0", "1kHz", 1],
...     ["log scale", "1kHz", "0.1GHz", 10],
...     ["linear scale", "0.1GHz", "10GHz", "0.1GHz"],
...     ])
```

Provide base simulation setup.

Set frequency list to the sweep frequencies.

# Parameters

frequency_list
: List of lists with four elements. The default is `None`. If provided, each list must contain:

  : 1 - frequency type (`"linear count"`, `"log scale"`, or `"linear scale"`)
    2 - start frequency
    3 - stop frequency
    4 - step frequency or count

# Returns

bool

: `True` if correctly executed, `False` otherwise.

Manages EDB methods for a frequency sweep.

# Parameters

sim_setup : {class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.siwave_simulation_setup_data.SiwaveSYZSimulationSetup`
name : str, optional

> Name of the frequency sweep.

edb_object

: EDB object. The default is `None`.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.return_current_distribution
documentation:
Whether to enable the return current distribution.
This option is used to accurately model the change of the characteristic impedance
of transmission lines caused by a discontinuous ground plane. Instead of injecting
the return current of a trace into a single point on the ground plane,
the return current for a high impedance trace is spread out.
The trace return current is not distributed when all traces attached to a node
have a characteristic impedance less than 75 ohms or if the difference between
two connected traces is less than 25 ohms.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if return current distribution is used, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.ignore_non_functional_pads
documentation:
Exclude non-functional pads.

# Returns

bool

: `` True` `` if functional pads have to be ignored, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.include_coplane_coupling
documentation:
Whether to enable coupling between traces and adjacent plane edges.
This option provides a model for crosstalk between signal lines and planes.
Plane edges couple to traces when they are parallel.
Traces and coplanar edges that are oblique to each other do not overlap
and cannot be considered for coupling.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if coplane coupling is used, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.include_fringe_coupling
documentation:
Whether to include the effect of fringe field coupling between stacked cavities.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if fringe coupling is used, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.include_split_plane_coupling
documentation:
Whether to account for coupling between adjacent parallel plane edges.
Primarily, two different cases are being considered:
\- Plane edges that form a split.
\- Plane edges that form a narrow trace-like plane.
The former leads to crosstalk between adjacent planes for which
a specific coupling model is applied. For the latter, fringing effects
are considered to model accurately the propagation characteristics
of trace-like cavities. Further, the coupling between narrow planes is
also modeled by enabling this feature.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if split plane coupling is used, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.include_infinite_ground
documentation:
Whether to Include a ground plane to serve as a voltage reference for traces and planes
if they are not defined in the layout.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if infinite ground is used, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.include_trace_coupling
documentation:
Whether to model coupling between adjacent traces.
Coupling is considered for parallel and almost parallel trace segments.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if trace coupling is used, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.include_vi_sources
documentation:
Whether to include the effect of parasitic elements from voltage and

# Returns

bool

: `True` if vi sources is used, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.sim_setup_data.io.siwave.AdvancedSettings.perform_erc
documentation:
Whether to perform an electrical rule check while generating the solver input.
In some designs, the same net may be divided into multiple nets with separate names.
These nets are connected at a "star" point. To simulate these nets, the error checking
for DC shorts must be turned off. All overlapping nets are then internally united
during simulation.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if perform erc is used, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.materials.Materials.add_djordjevicsarkar_dielectric
documentation:
Add a dielectric using the Djordjevic-Sarkar model.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the dielectric.

permittivity_at_frequency

: Relative permittivity of the dielectric.

loss_tangent_at_frequency

: Loss tangent for the material.

dielectric_model_frequency

: Test frequency in GHz for the dielectric.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.materials.Material`

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.materials.Materials.add_debye_material
documentation:
Add a dielectric with the Debye model.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the dielectric.

permittivity_low

: Relative permittivity of the dielectric at the frequency specified
  for `lower_frequency`.

permittivity_high

: Relative permittivity of the dielectric at the frequency specified
  for `higher_frequency`.

loss_tangent_low

: Loss tangent for the material at the frequency specified
  for `lower_frequency`.

loss_tangent_high

: Loss tangent for the material at the frequency specified
  for `higher_frequency`.

lower_freqency

: Value for the lower frequency.

higher_frequency

: Value for the higher frequency.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.materials.Material`

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.materials.Materials.add_multipole_debye_material
documentation:
Add a dielectric with the Multipole Debye model.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the dielectric.

frequencies

: Frequencies in GHz.

permittivities

: Relative permittivities at each frequency.

loss_tangents

: Loss tangents at each frequency.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.materials.Material`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edb = Edb()
>>> freq = [0, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
>>> rel_perm = [1e9, 1.1e9, 1.2e9, 1.3e9, 1.5e9, 1.6e9]
>>> loss_tan = [0.025, 0.026, 0.027, 0.028, 0.029, 0.030]
>>> diel = edb.materials.add_multipole_debye_material("My_MP_Debye", freq, rel_perm, loss_tan)
```

functions.name: pyedb.generic.data_handlers.from_rkm
documentation:
Convert an RKM code string to a string with a decimal point.

# Parameters

code

: RKM code string.

# Returns

str

: String with a decimal point and an R value.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('R47')
'0.47'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('4R7')
'4.7'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('470R')
'470'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('4K7')
'4.7k'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('47K')
'47k'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('47K3')
'47.3k'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('470K')
'470k'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('4M7')
'4.7M'
```

functions.name: pyedb.generic.data_handlers.str_to_bool
documentation:
Convert a `"True"` or `"False"` string to its corresponding Boolean value.

If the passed arguments are not relevant in the context of conversion, the argument
itself is returned. This method can be called using the `map()` function to
ensure conversion of Boolean strings in a list.

# Parameters

s: str

# Returns

bool or str

: The method is not case-sensitive.
  \- `True` is returned if the input is `"true"`, `"1"`,

  > `` "yes"` ``, or `"y"`,

  - `False` is returned if the input is `"false"`, `"no"`,
    `"n`, or `"0"`.
  - Otherwise, the input value is passed through the method unchanged.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.padstack.EdbPadstacks.create_coax_port
documentation:
Create HFSS 3Dlayout coaxial lumped port on a pastack
Requires to have solder ball defined before calling this method.

# Parameters

padstackinstance

: Padstack instance object.

use_dot_separator

: Whether to use `.` as the separator for the naming convention, which
  is `[component][net][pin]`. The default is `True`. If `False`, `_` is
  used as the separator instead.

name

: Port name for overwriting the default port-naming convention,
  which is `[component][net][pin]`. The port name must be unique.
  If a port with the specified name already exists, the
  default naming convention is used so that port creation does
  not fail.

# Returns

str

: Terminal name.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.padstack.EdbPadstacks.create_padstack
documentation:
Create a padstack.

:::{deprecated} 0.6.62
:::

Use {func}`create` method instead.

# Parameters

padstackname

: Name of the padstack. The default is `None`.

holediam

: Diameter of the hole with units. The default is `"300um"`.

paddiam

: Diameter of the pad with units. The default is `"400um"`.

antipaddiam

: Diameter of the antipad with units. The default is `"600um"`.

startlayer

: Starting layer. The default is `None`, in which case the top
  is the starting layer.

endlayer

: Ending layer. The default is `None`, in which case the bottom
  is the ending layer.

antipad_shape

: Shape of the antipad. The default is `"Circle"`. Options are `"Circle"` and `"Bullet"`.

x_size

: Only applicable to bullet shape. The default is `"600um"`.

y_size

: Only applicable to bullet shape. The default is `"600um"`.

corner_radius :

: Only applicable to bullet shape. The default is `"300um"`.

offset_x

: X offset of antipad. The default is `"0.0"`.

offset_y

: Y offset of antipad. The default is `"0.0"`.

rotation

: rotation of antipad. The default is `"0.0"`.

has_hole

: Whether this padstack has a hole.

# Returns

str

: Name of the padstack if the operation is successful.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.padstack.EdbPadstacks.create
documentation:
Create a padstack.

# Parameters

padstackname

: Name of the padstack. The default is `None`.

holediam

: Diameter of the hole with units. The default is `"300um"`.

paddiam

: Diameter of the pad with units, used with `"Circle"` shape. The default is `"400um"`.

antipaddiam

: Diameter of the antipad with units. The default is `"600um"`.

pad_shape

: Shape of the pad. The default is `"Circle`. Options are `"Circle"`, `"Rectangle"` and `"Polygon"`.

antipad_shape

: Shape of the antipad. The default is `"Circle"`. Options are `"Circle"` `"Rectangle"` and
  `"Bullet"`.

x_size

: Only applicable to bullet and rectangle shape. The default is `"600um"`.

y_size

: Only applicable to bullet and rectangle shape. The default is `"600um"`.

corner_radius :

: Only applicable to bullet shape. The default is `"300um"`.

offset_x

: X offset of antipad. The default is `"0.0"`.

offset_y

: Y offset of antipad. The default is `"0.0"`.

rotation

: rotation of antipad. The default is `"0.0"`.

has_hole

: Whether this padstack has a hole.

pad_offset_x

: Padstack offset in X direction.

pad_offset_y

: Padstack offset in Y direction.

pad_rotation

: Padstack rotation.

start_layer

: Start layer of the padstack definition.

stop_layer

: Stop layer of the padstack definition.

add_default_layer

: Add `"Default"` to padstack definition. Default is `False`.

anti_pad_x_size

: Only applicable to bullet and rectangle shape. The default is `"600um"`.

anti_pad_y_size

: Only applicable to bullet and rectangle shape. The default is `"600um"`.

hole_range

: Define the padstack hole range. Arguments supported, `"through"`, `"begin_on_upper_pad"`,
  `"end_on_lower_pad"`, `"upper_pad_to_lower_pad"`.

# Returns

str

: Name of the padstack if the operation is successful.

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.padstack.EdbPadstacks.get_reference_pins
documentation:
Search for reference pins using given criteria.

# Parameters

positive_pin

: Pin used for evaluating the distance on the reference pins found.

reference_net

: Reference net. The default is `"gnd"`.

search_radius

: Search radius for finding padstack instances. The default is `5e-3`.

max_limit

: Maximum limit for the padstack instances found. The default is `0`, in which
  case no limit is applied. The maximum limit value occurs on the nearest
  reference pins from the positive one that is found.

component_only

: Whether to limit the search to component padstack instances only. The
  default is `True`. When `False`, the search is extended to the entire layout.

# Returns

list

: List of {class}`dotnet.edb_core.edb_data.padstacks_data.EDBPadstackInstance`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> edbapp = Edb("target_path")
>>> pin = edbapp.components.instances["J5"].pins["19"]
>>> reference_pins = edbapp.padstacks.get_reference_pins(positive_pin=pin, reference_net="GND",
>>> search_radius=5e-3, max_limit=0, component_only=True)
```

methods.name: pyedb.dotnet.edb_core.padstack.EdbPadstacks.merge_via_along_lines
documentation:
Replace padstack instances along lines into a single polygon.

Detect all padstack instances that are placed along lines and replace them by a single polygon based one
forming a wall shape. This method is designed to simplify meshing on via fence usually added to shield RF traces
on PCB.

# Parameters

net_name

: Net name used for detected padstack instances. Default value is `"GND"`.

distance_threshold

: If two points in a line are separated by a distance larger than `distance_threshold`,
  the line is divided in two parts. Default is `5e-3` (5mm), in which case the control is not performed.

minimum_via_number

: The minimum number of points that a line must contain. Default is `6`.

selected_angles

: Specify angle in degrees to detected, for instance [0, 180] is only detecting horizontal and vertical lines.
  Other values can be assigned like 45 degrees. When `None` is provided all lines are detected. Default value
  is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when succeeded `False` when failed. \<

Manages EDB methods for nets management accessible from `Edb.padstacks` property.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> edbapp = Edb("myaedbfolder", edbversion="2021.2")
>>> edb_padstacks = edbapp.padstacks
```

functions.name: pyedb.generic.design_types.Edb
documentation:
Provides the EDB application interface.

This module inherits all objects that belong to EDB.

# Parameters

edbpath

: Full path to the `aedb` folder. The variable can also contain
  the path to a layout to import. Allowed formats are BRD,
  XML (IPC2581), GDS, and DXF. The default is `None`.
  For GDS import, the Ansys control file (also XML) should have the same
  name as the GDS file. Only the file extension differs.

cellname

: Name of the cell to select. The default is `None`.

isreadonly

: Whether to open EBD in read-only mode when it is
  owned by HFSS 3D Layout. The default is `False`.

edbversion

: Version of EDB to use. The default is `"2021.2"`.

isaedtowned

: Whether to launch EDB from HFSS 3D Layout. The
  default is `False`.

oproject

: Reference to the AEDT project object.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False.`

technology_file

: Full path to technology file to be converted to xml before importing or xml. Supported by GDS format only.

# Returns

{class}`pyedb.dotnet.edb.Edb`, {class}`pyedb.grpc.edb.Edb`

# Examples

Create an `Edb` object and a new EDB cell.

```pycon
>>> from pyedb import Edb
>>> app = Edb()
```

Add a new variable named "s1" to the `Edb` instance.

```pycon
>>> app['s1'] = "0.25 mm"
>>> app['s1'].tofloat
>>> 0.00025
>>> app['s1'].tostring
>>> "0.25mm"
```

or add a new parameter with description:

```pycon
>>> app['s2'] = ["20um", "Spacing between traces"]
>>> app['s2'].value
>>> 1.9999999999999998e-05
>>> app['s2'].description
>>> 'Spacing between traces'
```

Create an `Edb` object and open the specified project.

```pycon
>>> app = Edb("myfile.aedb")
```

Create an `Edb` object from GDS and control files.
The XML control file resides in the same directory as the GDS file: (myfile.xml).

```pycon
>>> app = Edb("/path/to/file/myfile.gds")
```

functions.name: pyedb.generic.constants.unit_system
documentation:
Retrieve the name of the unit system associated with a unit string.

# Parameters

units

: Units for retrieving the associated unit system name.

# Returns

str

: Key from the `AEDT_units` when successful. For example, `"AngularSpeed"`.

`False` when the units specified are not defined in AEDT units.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.constants._resolve_unit_system
documentation:
Retrieve the unit string of an arithmetic operation on `Variable` objects. If no resulting unit system
is defined for a specific operation (in unit_system_operations), an empty string is returned

# Parameters

unit_system_1

: Name of a unit system, which is a key of `AEDT_units`.

unit_system_2

: Name of another unit system, which is a key of `AEDT_units`.

operation

: Name of an operator within the data set of `["multiply" ,"divide"]`.

# Returns

str

: Unit system when successful, `""` when failed.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.constants.unit_converter
documentation:
Convert unit in specified unit system.

Parameters
----------
values : float, list
    Values to convert.
unit_system : str
    Unit system. Default is `"Length"`.
input_units : str
    Input units. Default is `"meter"`.
output_units : str
    Output units. Default is `"mm"`.

Returns
-------
float, list
    Converted value.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.constants.scale_units
documentation:
Find the scale_to_unit into main system unit.

Parameters
----------
scale_to_unit : str
    Unit to Scale.

Returns
-------
float
    Return the scaling factor if any.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.constants.validate_enum_class_value

cls

value

: Value to check.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when the value is valid for the `enumeration-class`, `False` otherwise.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.plot.plot_matplotlib
documentation:
Create a matplotlib plot based on a list of data.

# Parameters

plot_data

: List of plot data. Every item has to be in the following format
  For type `fill`: `[x points, y points, color, label, alpha, type=="fill"]`.
  For type `path`: `[vertices, codes, color, label, alpha, type=="path"]`.
  For type `contour`: `[vertices, codes, color, label, alpha, line_width, type=="contour"]`.

size

: Image size in pixel (width, height). Default is `(2000, 1000)`.

show_legend

: Either to show legend or not. Default is `True`.

xlabel

: Plot X label. Default is `""`.

ylabel

: Plot Y label. Default is `""`.

title

: Plot Title label. Default is `""`.

save_plot

: If a path is specified the plot will be saved in this location.
  If `save_plot` is provided, the `show` parameter is ignored.

x_limits

: List of x limits (left and right). Default is `None`.

y_limits

: List of y limits (bottom and top). Default is `None`.

axis_equal

: > Whether to show the same scale on both axis or have a different scale based on plot size.

  Default is `False`.

annotations

: List of annotations to add to the plot. The format is [x, y, string, dictionary of font options].
  Default is `None`.

show

: Whether to show the plot or return the matplotlib object. Default is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.plt`

: Matplotlib fig object.

evaluating a bounding box by exporting a SAT file is disabled.

Flag for enabling and disabling the global PyEDB log file located in the global temp folder.

methods.name: pyedb.configuration.configuration.Configuration.load
documentation:
Import configuration settings from a configure file.

# Parameters

config_file

: Full path to configure file in JSON or TOML format. Dictionary is also supported.

append

: Whether if the new file will append to existing properties or the properties will be cleared before import.
  Default is `True` to keep stored properties

apply_file

: Whether to apply the file after the load or not. Default is `False`.

output_file

: Full path to the new aedb folder where the configured project will be saved.

open_at_the_end

: Whether to keep the new generated file opened at the end. Default is `True`.

# Returns

dict

: Config dictionary.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.generate_unique_project_name
documentation:
Generate a new AEDT project name given a rootname.

: Root name where the new project is to be created.

folder_name

: Name of the folder to create. The default is `None`, in which case a random folder
  is created. Use `""` if you do not want to create a subfolder.

project_name

: Name for the project. The default is `None`, in which case a random project is
  created. If a project with this name already exists, a new suffix is added.

project_format

: Project format. The default is `"aedt"`. Options are `"aedt"` and `"aedb"`.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.filter_tuple
documentation: Filter a tuple of two elements with two search keywords.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.filter_string
documentation: Filter a string

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.recursive_glob
documentation:
Get a list of files matching a pattern, searching recursively from a start path.

Keyword Arguments:
startpath -- starting path (directory)
filepattern -- fnmatch-style filename pattern

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.number_aware_string_key
documentation:
Get a key for sorting strings that treats embedded digit sequences as integers.

Parameters
----------
s : str
    String to calculate the key from.

Returns
-------
tuple
    Tuple of key entries.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.compute_fft
documentation:
Compute FFT of input transient data.

Parameters
----------
time_vals : `pandas.Series`
value : `pandas.Series`

Returns
-------
tuple
    Frequency and Values.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.parse_excitation_file
documentation:
Parse a csv file and convert data in list that can be applied to Hfss and Hfss3dLayout sources.

Parameters
----------
file_name : str
    Full name of the input file.
is_time_domain : bool, optional
    Either if the input data is Time based or Frequency Based. Frequency based data are Mag/Phase (deg).
x_scale : float, optional
    Scaling factor for x axis.
y_scale : float, optional
    Scaling factor for y axis.
data_format : str, optional
    Either `"Power"`, `"Current"` or `"Voltage"`.
impedance : float, optional
    Excitation impedance. Default is `50`.
encoding : str, optional
    Csv file encoding.
out_mag : str, optional
    Output magnitude format. It can be `"Voltage"` or `"Power"` depending on Hfss solution.

Returns
-------
tuple
    Frequency, magnitude and phase.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.tech_to_control_file
documentation:
Convert a TECH file to an XML file for use in a GDS or DXF import.

# Parameters

tech_path

: Full path to the TECH file.

unit

: Tech units. If specified in tech file this parameter will not be used. Default is `"nm"`.

control_path

: Path for outputting the XML file.

# Returns

str

: Out xml file.

functions.name: pyedb.generic.general_methods.install_with_pip
documentation:
Install a new package using pip.
This method is useful for installing a package from the AEDT Console without launching the Python environment.

# Parameters

package_name

: Name of the package to install.

package_path

: Path for the GitHub package to download and install. For example, `git+https://.....`.

upgrade

: Whether to upgrade the package. The default is `False`.

uninstall

: Whether to install the package or uninstall the package.

methods.name: pyedb.siwave_core.icepak.Icepak.set_board_outline_fidelity

Specifies the minimum edge length when modifying the board outline for export to Icepak. This

simplifying the outline to remove very small edges.

methods.name: pyedb.siwave_core.icepak.Icepak.set_thermal_environment
documentation:
Sets the thermal environment settings to use for Icepak simulations.

Parameters
----------
convection : bool, optional
force_air : bool, optional
top_or_ambient_temperature: int, float, optional
    Temperature above PCB in degrees Celsius.
top_or_overall_flow_direction : str, optional
    Flow direction above PCB.
top_or_overall_flow_speed : int, float, optional
    Flow speed above PCB.
bottom_temperature : int, float, optional
    Temperature below PCB in degrees Celsius.
bottom_flow_direction : str, optional
    Flow direction below PCB.
bottom_flow_speed : int, float, optional
    Flow speed below PCB.
gravity_vector_x : int, float, optional
    Gravity vector x for natural convection.
gravity_vector_y : int, float, optional
    Gravity vector y for natural convection.
gravity_vector_z : int, float, optional
    Gravity vector z for natural convection.

-------

functions.name: pyedb.misc.downloads.download_aedb
documentation:
Download an example of AEDB File and return the def path.

Examples files are downloaded to a persistent cache to avoid
re-downloading the same file twice.

# Parameters

destination

: Path for downloading files. The default is the user's temp folder.

# Returns

str

: Path to the example file.

# Examples

Download an example result file and return the path of the file.
\>>> import pyedb.misc.downloads
\>>> path = pyedb.misc.downloads.download_aedb()
\>>> path
'C:/Users/user/AppData/local/temp/Galileo.aedb'
front-facing functions

functions.name: pyedb.misc.downloads.download_edb_merge_utility
documentation:
Download an example of WPF Project which allows to merge 2aedb files.

Examples files are downloaded to a persistent cache to avoid
re-downloading the same file twice.

# Parameters

force_download

: Force to delete cache and download files again.

destination

: Path for downloading files. The default is the user's temp folder.

# Returns

str

: Path to the example file.

# Examples

Download an example result file and return the path of the file.
\>>> import pyedb.misc.downloads
\>>> path = pyedb.misc.downloads.download_edb_merge_utility(force_download=True)
\>>> path
'C:/Users/user/AppData/local/temp/wpf_edb_merge/merge_wizard.py'

functions.name: pyedb.misc.downloads.download_via_wizard
documentation:
Download an example of Hfss Via Wizard and return the def path.

Examples files are downloaded to a persistent cache to avoid
re-downloading the same file twice.

# Parameters

destination

: Path for downloading files. The default is the user's temp folder.

# Returns

str

: Path to the example file.

# Examples

Download an example result file and return the path of the file.

```pycon
>>> import pyedb.misc.downloads
>>> path = pyedb.misc.downloads.download_via_wizard()
>>> path
'C:/Users/user/AppData/local/temp/pyaedtexamples/Graphic_Card.aedt'
```

functions.name: pyedb.misc.downloads.download_file
documentation:
Download file from directory.

Files are downloaded to a destination. If filename is not specified, the full directory will be downloaded.

# Parameters

directory

: Directory name.

filename

: File name to download. The default is all files inside directory.

destination

: Path where files will be downloaded. Default is user temp folder.

# Returns

str

: Path to the example file.

# Examples

Download an example result file and return the path of the file.

```pycon
>>> import pyedb.misc.downloads
>>> path = pyedb.misc.downloads.download_file("motorcad", "IPM_Vweb_Hairpin.mot")
>>> path
'C:/Users/user/AppData/local/temp/PyAEDTExamples/motorcad'
```
